texts.orig: (c) the recipients or categories of recipient to whom the personal data have been or will be disclosed, in particular recipients in third countries or international organisations;
texts.text: (c) the recipients or categories of recipient to whom the personal data have been or will be disclosed, in particular recipients in third countries or international organisations;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) where possible, the envisaged period for which the personal data will be stored, or, if not possible, the criteria used to determine that period;
texts.text: (d) where possible, the envisaged period for which the personal data will be stored, or, if not possible, the criteria used to determine that period;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) the existence of the right to request from the controller rectification or erasure of personal data or restriction of processing of personal data concerning the data subject or to object to such processing;
texts.text: (e) the existence of the right to request from the controller rectification or erasure of personal data or restriction of processing of personal data concerning the data subject or to object to such processing;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f) the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority;
texts.text: (f) the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (g) where the personal data are not collected from the data subject, any available information as to their source;
texts.text: (g) where the personal data are not collected from the data subject, any available information as to their source;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (h) the existence of automated decision-making, including profiling, referred to in Article 22(1) and (4) and, at least in those cases, meaningful information about the logic involved, as well as the significance and the envisaged consequences of such processing for the data subject.
texts.text: (h) the existence of automated decision-making, including profiling, referred to in Article 22(1) and (4) and, at least in those cases, meaningful information about the logic involved, as well as the significance and the envisaged consequences of such processing for the data subject.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where personal data are transferred to a third country or to an international organisation, the data subject shall have the right to be informed of the appropriate safeguards pursuant to Article 46 relating to the transfer.
texts.text: Where personal data are transferred to a third country or to an international organisation, the data subject shall have the right to be informed of the appropriate safeguards pursuant to Article 46 relating to the transfer.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The controller shall provide a copy of the personal data undergoing processing. For any further copies requested by the data subject, the controller may charge a reasonable fee based on administrative costs. Where the data subject makes the request by electronic means, and unless otherwise requested by the data subject, the information shall be provided in a commonly used electronic form.
texts.text: The controller shall provide a copy of the personal data undergoing processing. For any further copies requested by the data subject, the controller may charge a reasonable fee based on administrative costs. Where the data subject makes the request by electronic means, and unless otherwise requested by the data subject, the information shall be provided in a commonly used electronic form.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The right to obtain a copy referred to in paragraph 3 shall not adversely affect the rights and freedoms of others.
texts.text: The right to obtain a copy referred to in paragraph 3 shall not adversely affect the rights and freedoms of others.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Section 3
texts.text: Section 3

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rectification and erasure
texts.text: Rectification and erasure

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 16
texts.text: Article 16

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Right to rectification
texts.text: Right to rectification

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  data  subject  shall  have  the  right  to  obtain  from  the  controller  without  undue  delay  the  rectification  of  inaccurate personal data concerning him or her. Taking into account the purposes of  the processing, the data subject shall have the right to have incomplete personal data completed, including by means of providing a supplementary statement.
texts.text: The  data  subject  shall  have  the  right  to  obtain  from  the  controller  without  undue  delay  the  rectification  of  inaccurate personal data concerning him or her. Taking into account the purposes of  the processing, the data subject shall have the right to have incomplete personal data completed, including by means of providing a supplementary statement.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 17
texts.text: Article 17

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Right to erasure ('right to be forgotten')
texts.text: Right to erasure ('right to be forgotten')

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller the erasure of personal data concerning him or her without undue delay and the controller shall have the obligation to erase personal data without undue delay where one of the following grounds applies:
texts.text: The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller the erasure of personal data concerning him or her without undue delay and the controller shall have the obligation to erase personal data without undue delay where one of the following grounds applies:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) the personal data are no longer necessary in relation to the purposes for which they were collected or otherwise processed;
texts.text: (a) the personal data are no longer necessary in relation to the purposes for which they were collected or otherwise processed;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) the data subject withdraws consent on which the processing is based according to point (a) of Article 6(1), or point (a) of Article 9(2), and where there is no other legal ground for the processing;
texts.text: (b) the data subject withdraws consent on which the processing is based according to point (a) of Article 6(1), or point (a) of Article 9(2), and where there is no other legal ground for the processing;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) the data subject objects to the processing pursuant to Article 21(1) and there are no overriding legitimate grounds for the processing, or the data subject objects to the processing pursuant to Article 21(2);
texts.text: (c) the data subject objects to the processing pursuant to Article 21(1) and there are no overriding legitimate grounds for the processing, or the data subject objects to the processing pursuant to Article 21(2);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) the personal data have been unlawfully processed;
texts.text: (d) the personal data have been unlawfully processed;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) the personal data have to be erased for compliance with a legal obligation in Union or Member State law to which the controller is subject;
texts.text: (e) the personal data have to be erased for compliance with a legal obligation in Union or Member State law to which the controller is subject;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f) the personal data have been collected in relation to the offer of information society services referred to in Article 8(1).
texts.text: (f) the personal data have been collected in relation to the offer of information society services referred to in Article 8(1).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where the controller has made the personal data public and is obliged pursuant to paragraph 1 to erase the personal data, the controller, taking account of available technology and the cost of implementation, shall take reasonable steps, including technical measures, to inform controllers which are processing the personal data that the data subject has requested the erasure by such controllers of any links to, or copy or replication of, those personal data.
texts.text: Where the controller has made the personal data public and is obliged pursuant to paragraph 1 to erase the personal data, the controller, taking account of available technology and the cost of implementation, shall take reasonable steps, including technical measures, to inform controllers which are processing the personal data that the data subject has requested the erasure by such controllers of any links to, or copy or replication of, those personal data.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Paragraphs 1 and 2 shall not apply to the extent that processing is necessary:
texts.text: Paragraphs 1 and 2 shall not apply to the extent that processing is necessary:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) for exercising the right of freedom of expression and information;
texts.text: (a) for exercising the right of freedom of expression and information;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) for compliance with a legal obligation which requires processing by Union or Member State law to which the controller is subject or for the performance of a task carried out in the public interest or in the exercise of official authority vested in the controller;
texts.text: (b) for compliance with a legal obligation which requires processing by Union or Member State law to which the controller is subject or for the performance of a task carried out in the public interest or in the exercise of official authority vested in the controller;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) for reasons of public interest in the area of public health in accordance with points (h) and (i) of Article 9(2) as well as Article 9(3);
texts.text: (c) for reasons of public interest in the area of public health in accordance with points (h) and (i) of Article 9(2) as well as Article 9(3);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes in accordance with Article 89(1) in so far as the right referred to in paragraph 1 is likely to render impossible or seriously impair the achievement of the objectives of that processing; or
texts.text: (d) for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes in accordance with Article 89(1) in so far as the right referred to in paragraph 1 is likely to render impossible or seriously impair the achievement of the objectives of that processing; or

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) for the establishment, exercise or defence of legal claims.
texts.text: (e) for the establishment, exercise or defence of legal claims.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 18
texts.text: Article 18

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Right to restriction of processing
texts.text: Right to restriction of processing

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller restriction of processing where one of the following applies:
texts.text: The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller restriction of processing where one of the following applies:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) the accuracy of the personal data is contested by the data subject, for a period enabling the controller to verify the accuracy of the personal data;
texts.text: (a) the accuracy of the personal data is contested by the data subject, for a period enabling the controller to verify the accuracy of the personal data;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) the processing is unlawful and the data subject opposes the erasure of the personal data and requests the restriction of their use instead;
texts.text: (b) the processing is unlawful and the data subject opposes the erasure of the personal data and requests the restriction of their use instead;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) the controller no longer needs the personal data for the purposes of the processing, but they are required by the data subject for the establishment, exercise or defence of legal claims;
texts.text: (c) the controller no longer needs the personal data for the purposes of the processing, but they are required by the data subject for the establishment, exercise or defence of legal claims;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) the data subject has objected to processing pursuant to Article 21(1) pending the verification whether the legitimate grounds of the controller override those of the data subject.
texts.text: (d) the data subject has objected to processing pursuant to Article 21(1) pending the verification whether the legitimate grounds of the controller override those of the data subject.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where processing has been restricted under paragraph 1, such personal data shall, with the exception of storage, only be processed with the data subject's consent or for the establishment, exercise or defence of legal claims or for the protection of the rights of another natural or legal person or for reasons of important public interest of the Union or of a Member State.
texts.text: Where processing has been restricted under paragraph 1, such personal data shall, with the exception of storage, only be processed with the data subject's consent or for the establishment, exercise or defence of legal claims or for the protection of the rights of another natural or legal person or for reasons of important public interest of the Union or of a Member State.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A data subject who has obtained restriction of processing pursuant to paragraph 1 shall be informed by the controller before the restriction of processing is lifted.
texts.text: A data subject who has obtained restriction of processing pursuant to paragraph 1 shall be informed by the controller before the restriction of processing is lifted.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 19
texts.text: Article 19

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Notification obligation regarding rectification or erasure of personal data or restriction of processing
texts.text: Notification obligation regarding rectification or erasure of personal data or restriction of processing

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The controller shall communicate any rectification or erasure of personal data or  restriction of processing carried out in accordance  with  Article  16,  Article  17(1)  and  Article  18  to  each  recipient  to  whom  the  personal  data  have  been disclosed,  unless  this  proves  impossible  or  involves  disproportionate  effort.  The  controller  shall  inform  the  data  subject about those recipients if the data subject requests it.
texts.text: The controller shall communicate any rectification or erasure of personal data or  restriction of processing carried out in accordance  with  Article  16,  Article  17(1)  and  Article  18  to  each  recipient  to  whom  the  personal  data  have  been disclosed,  unless  this  proves  impossible  or  involves  disproportionate  effort.  The  controller  shall  inform  the  data  subject about those recipients if the data subject requests it.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 20
texts.text: Article 20

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Right to data portability
texts.text: Right to data portability

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The data subject shall have the right to receive the personal data concerning him or her, which he or she has provided to a controller, in a structured, commonly used and machine-readable format and have the right to transmit those data to another controller without hindrance from the controller to which the personal data have been provided, where:
texts.text: The data subject shall have the right to receive the personal data concerning him or her, which he or she has provided to a controller, in a structured, commonly used and machine-readable format and have the right to transmit those data to another controller without hindrance from the controller to which the personal data have been provided, where:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) the processing is based on consent pursuant to point (a) of Article 6(1) or point (a) of Article 9(2) or on a contract pursuant to point (b) of Article 6(1); and
texts.text: (a) the processing is based on consent pursuant to point (a) of Article 6(1) or point (a) of Article 9(2) or on a contract pursuant to point (b) of Article 6(1); and

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) the processing is carried out by automated means.
texts.text: (b) the processing is carried out by automated means.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In exercising his or her right to data portability pursuant to paragraph 1, the data subject shall have the right to have the personal data transmitted directly from one controller to another, where technically feasible.
texts.text: In exercising his or her right to data portability pursuant to paragraph 1, the data subject shall have the right to have the personal data transmitted directly from one controller to another, where technically feasible.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The exercise of the right referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article shall be without prejudice to Article 17. That right shall not apply to processing necessary for the performance of a task carried out in the public interest or in the exercise of official authority vested in the controller.
texts.text: The exercise of the right referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article shall be without prejudice to Article 17. That right shall not apply to processing necessary for the performance of a task carried out in the public interest or in the exercise of official authority vested in the controller.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The right referred to in paragraph 1 shall not adversely affect the rights and freedoms of others.
texts.text: The right referred to in paragraph 1 shall not adversely affect the rights and freedoms of others.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Section 4
texts.text: Section 4

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Right to object and automated individual decision-making
texts.text: Right to object and automated individual decision-making

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 21
texts.text: Article 21

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Right to object
texts.text: Right to object

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The data subject shall have the right to object, on grounds relating to his or her particular situation, at any time to processing of personal data concerning him or her which is based on point (e) or (f) of Article 6(1), including profiling based on those provisions. The controller shall no longer process the personal data unless the controller demonstrates compelling legitimate grounds for the processing which override the interests, rights and freedoms of the data subject or for the establishment, exercise or defence of legal claims.
texts.text: The data subject shall have the right to object, on grounds relating to his or her particular situation, at any time to processing of personal data concerning him or her which is based on point (e) or (f) of Article 6(1), including profiling based on those provisions. The controller shall no longer process the personal data unless the controller demonstrates compelling legitimate grounds for the processing which override the interests, rights and freedoms of the data subject or for the establishment, exercise or defence of legal claims.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where personal data are processed for direct marketing purposes, the data subject shall have the right to object at any time to processing of personal data concerning him or her for such marketing, which includes profiling to the extent that it is related to such direct marketing.
texts.text: Where personal data are processed for direct marketing purposes, the data subject shall have the right to object at any time to processing of personal data concerning him or her for such marketing, which includes profiling to the extent that it is related to such direct marketing.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where the data subject objects to processing for direct marketing purposes, the personal data shall no longer be processed for such purposes.
texts.text: Where the data subject objects to processing for direct marketing purposes, the personal data shall no longer be processed for such purposes.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: At the latest at the time of the first communication with the data subject, the right referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2 shall be explicitly brought to the attention of the data subject and shall be presented clearly and separately from any other information.
texts.text: At the latest at the time of the first communication with the data subject, the right referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2 shall be explicitly brought to the attention of the data subject and shall be presented clearly and separately from any other information.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In the context of the use of information society services, and notwithstanding Directive 2002/58/EC, the data subject may exercise his or her right to object by automated means using technical specifications.
texts.text: In the context of the use of information society services, and notwithstanding Directive 2002/58/EC, the data subject may exercise his or her right to object by automated means using technical specifications.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where personal data are processed for scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes pursuant to Article 89(1), the data subject, on grounds relating to his or her particular situation, shall have the right to object to processing of personal data concerning him or her, unless the processing is necessary for the performance of a task carried out for reasons of public interest.
texts.text: Where personal data are processed for scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes pursuant to Article 89(1), the data subject, on grounds relating to his or her particular situation, shall have the right to object to processing of personal data concerning him or her, unless the processing is necessary for the performance of a task carried out for reasons of public interest.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 22
texts.text: Article 22

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Automated individual decision-making, including profiling
texts.text: Automated individual decision-making, including profiling

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The data subject shall have the right not to be subject to a decision based solely on automated processing, including profiling, which produces legal effects concerning him or her or similarly significantly affects him or her.
texts.text: The data subject shall have the right not to be subject to a decision based solely on automated processing, including profiling, which produces legal effects concerning him or her or similarly significantly affects him or her.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Paragraph 1 shall not apply if the decision:
texts.text: Paragraph 1 shall not apply if the decision:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) is necessary for entering into, or performance of, a contract between the data subject and a data controller;
texts.text: (a) is necessary for entering into, or performance of, a contract between the data subject and a data controller;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) is authorised by Union or Member State law to which the controller is subject and which also lays down suitable measures to safeguard the data subject's rights and freedoms and legitimate interests; or
texts.text: (b) is authorised by Union or Member State law to which the controller is subject and which also lays down suitable measures to safeguard the data subject's rights and freedoms and legitimate interests; or

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) is based on the data subject's explicit consent.
texts.text: (c) is based on the data subject's explicit consent.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In the cases referred to in points (a) and (c) of paragraph 2, the data controller shall implement suitable measures to safeguard the data subject's rights and freedoms and legitimate interests, at least the right to obtain human intervention on the part of the controller, to express his or her point of view and to contest the decision.
texts.text: In the cases referred to in points (a) and (c) of paragraph 2, the data controller shall implement suitable measures to safeguard the data subject's rights and freedoms and legitimate interests, at least the right to obtain human intervention on the part of the controller, to express his or her point of view and to contest the decision.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Decisions referred to in paragraph 2 shall not be based on special categories of personal data referred to in Article 9(1), unless point (a) or (g) of Article 9(2) applies and suitable measures to safeguard the data subject's rights and freedoms and legitimate interests are in place.
texts.text: Decisions referred to in paragraph 2 shall not be based on special categories of personal data referred to in Article 9(1), unless point (a) or (g) of Article 9(2) applies and suitable measures to safeguard the data subject's rights and freedoms and legitimate interests are in place.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Section 5
texts.text: Section 5

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Restrictions
texts.text: Restrictions

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 23
texts.text: Article 23

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Restrictions
texts.text: Restrictions

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Union or Member State law to which the data controller or processor is subject may restrict by way of a legislative measure the scope of the obligations and rights provided for in Articles 12 to 22 and Article 34, as well as Article 5 in so far as its provisions correspond to the rights and obligations provided for in Articles 12 to 22, when such a restriction respects the essence of the fundamental rights and freedoms and is a necessary and proportionate measure in a democratic society to safeguard:
texts.text: Union or Member State law to which the data controller or processor is subject may restrict by way of a legislative measure the scope of the obligations and rights provided for in Articles 12 to 22 and Article 34, as well as Article 5 in so far as its provisions correspond to the rights and obligations provided for in Articles 12 to 22, when such a restriction respects the essence of the fundamental rights and freedoms and is a necessary and proportionate measure in a democratic society to safeguard:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) national security;
texts.text: (a) national security;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) defence;
texts.text: (b) defence;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) public security;
texts.text: (c) public security;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) the prevention, investigation, detection or prosecution of criminal offences or the execution of criminal penalties, including the safeguarding against and the prevention of threats to public security;
texts.text: (d) the prevention, investigation, detection or prosecution of criminal offences or the execution of criminal penalties, including the safeguarding against and the prevention of threats to public security;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) other important objectives of general public interest of the Union or of a Member State, in particular an important economic or financial interest of the Union or of a Member State, including monetary, budgetary and taxation a matters, public health and social security;
texts.text: (e) other important objectives of general public interest of the Union or of a Member State, in particular an important economic or financial interest of the Union or of a Member State, including monetary, budgetary and taxation a matters, public health and social security;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f) the protection of judicial independence and judicial proceedings;
texts.text: (f) the protection of judicial independence and judicial proceedings;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (g) the prevention, investigation, detection and prosecution of breaches of ethics for regulated professions;
texts.text: (g) the prevention, investigation, detection and prosecution of breaches of ethics for regulated professions;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (h) a monitoring, inspection or regulatory function connected, even occasionally, to the exercise of official authority in the cases referred to in points (a) to (e) and (g);
texts.text: (h) a monitoring, inspection or regulatory function connected, even occasionally, to the exercise of official authority in the cases referred to in points (a) to (e) and (g);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (i) the protection of the data subject or the rights and freedoms of others;
texts.text: (i) the protection of the data subject or the rights and freedoms of others;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (j) the enforcement of civil law claims.
texts.text: (j) the enforcement of civil law claims.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In particular, any legislative measure referred to in paragraph 1 shall contain specific provisions at least, where relevant, as to:
texts.text: In particular, any legislative measure referred to in paragraph 1 shall contain specific provisions at least, where relevant, as to:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) the purposes of the processing or categories of processing;
texts.text: (a) the purposes of the processing or categories of processing;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) the categories of personal data;
texts.text: (b) the categories of personal data;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) the scope of the restrictions introduced;
texts.text: (c) the scope of the restrictions introduced;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) the safeguards to prevent abuse or unlawful access or transfer;
texts.text: (d) the safeguards to prevent abuse or unlawful access or transfer;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) the specification of the controller or categories of controllers;
texts.text: (e) the specification of the controller or categories of controllers;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f) the storage periods and the applicable safeguards taking into account the nature, scope and purposes of the processing or categories of processing;
texts.text: (f) the storage periods and the applicable safeguards taking into account the nature, scope and purposes of the processing or categories of processing;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (g) the risks to the rights and freedoms of data subjects; and
texts.text: (g) the risks to the rights and freedoms of data subjects; and

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (h) the right of data subjects to be informed about the restriction, unless that may be prejudicial to the purpose of the restriction.
texts.text: (h) the right of data subjects to be informed about the restriction, unless that may be prejudicial to the purpose of the restriction.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: CHAPTER IV
texts.text: CHAPTER IV

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Controller and processor
texts.text: Controller and processor

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Section 1
texts.text: Section 1

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: General obligations
texts.text: General obligations

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 24
texts.text: Article 24

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Responsibility of the controller
texts.text: Responsibility of the controller

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Taking into account the nature, scope, context and purposes of processing as well as the risks of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure and to be able to demonstrate that processing is performed in accordance with this Regulation. Those measures shall be reviewed and updated where necessary.
texts.text: Taking into account the nature, scope, context and purposes of processing as well as the risks of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure and to be able to demonstrate that processing is performed in accordance with this Regulation. Those measures shall be reviewed and updated where necessary.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where proportionate in relation to processing activities, the measures referred to in paragraph 1 shall include the implementation of appropriate data protection policies by the controller.
texts.text: Where proportionate in relation to processing activities, the measures referred to in paragraph 1 shall include the implementation of appropriate data protection policies by the controller.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Adherence to approved codes of conduct as referred to in Article 40 or approved certification mechanisms as referred to in Article 42 may be used as an element by which to demonstrate compliance with the obligations of the controller.
texts.text: Adherence to approved codes of conduct as referred to in Article 40 or approved certification mechanisms as referred to in Article 42 may be used as an element by which to demonstrate compliance with the obligations of the controller.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 25
texts.text: Article 25

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Data protection by design and by default
texts.text: Data protection by design and by default

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Taking into account the state of the art, the cost of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of processing as well as the risks of varying likelihood and severity for rights and freedoms of natural persons posed by the processing, the controller shall, both at the time of the determination of the means for processing and at the time of the processing itself, implement appropriate technical and organisational measures, such as pseudonymisation, which are designed to implement data-protection principles, such as data minimisation, in an effective manner and to integrate the necessary safeguards into the processing in order to meet the requirements of this Regulation and protect the rights of data subjects.
texts.text: Taking into account the state of the art, the cost of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of processing as well as the risks of varying likelihood and severity for rights and freedoms of natural persons posed by the processing, the controller shall, both at the time of the determination of the means for processing and at the time of the processing itself, implement appropriate technical and organisational measures, such as pseudonymisation, which are designed to implement data-protection principles, such as data minimisation, in an effective manner and to integrate the necessary safeguards into the processing in order to meet the requirements of this Regulation and protect the rights of data subjects.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The controller shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures for ensuring that, by default, only personal data which are necessary for each specific purpose of the processing are processed. That obligation applies to the amount of personal data collected, the extent of their processing, the period of their storage and their accessibility. In particular, such measures shall ensure that by default personal data are not made accessible without the individual's intervention to an indefinite number of natural persons.
texts.text: The controller shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures for ensuring that, by default, only personal data which are necessary for each specific purpose of the processing are processed. That obligation applies to the amount of personal data collected, the extent of their processing, the period of their storage and their accessibility. In particular, such measures shall ensure that by default personal data are not made accessible without the individual's intervention to an indefinite number of natural persons.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: An approved certification mechanism pursuant to Article 42 may be used as an element to demonstrate compliance with the requirements set out in paragraphs 1 and 2 of this Article.
texts.text: An approved certification mechanism pursuant to Article 42 may be used as an element to demonstrate compliance with the requirements set out in paragraphs 1 and 2 of this Article.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 26
texts.text: Article 26

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Joint controllers
texts.text: Joint controllers

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where two or more controllers jointly determine the purposes and means of processing, they shall be joint controllers. They shall in a transparent manner determine their respective responsibilities for compliance with the obligations under this Regulation, in particular as regards the exercising of the rights of the data subject and their respective duties to provide the information referred to in Articles 13 and 14, by means of an arrangement between them unless, and in so far as, the respective responsibilities of the controllers are determined by Union or Member State law to which the controllers are subject. The arrangement may designate a contact point for data subjects.
texts.text: Where two or more controllers jointly determine the purposes and means of processing, they shall be joint controllers. They shall in a transparent manner determine their respective responsibilities for compliance with the obligations under this Regulation, in particular as regards the exercising of the rights of the data subject and their respective duties to provide the information referred to in Articles 13 and 14, by means of an arrangement between them unless, and in so far as, the respective responsibilities of the controllers are determined by Union or Member State law to which the controllers are subject. The arrangement may designate a contact point for data subjects.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The arrangement referred to in paragraph 1 shall duly reflect the respective roles and relationships of the joint controllers vis-à-vis the data subjects. The essence of the arrangement shall be made available to the data subject.
texts.text: The arrangement referred to in paragraph 1 shall duly reflect the respective roles and relationships of the joint controllers vis-à-vis the data subjects. The essence of the arrangement shall be made available to the data subject.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Irrespective of the terms of the arrangement referred to in paragraph 1, the data subject may exercise his or her rights under this Regulation in respect of and against each of the controllers.
texts.text: Irrespective of the terms of the arrangement referred to in paragraph 1, the data subject may exercise his or her rights under this Regulation in respect of and against each of the controllers.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 27
texts.text: Article 27

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Representatives of controllers or processors not established in the Union
texts.text: Representatives of controllers or processors not established in the Union

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where Article 3(2) applies, the controller or the processor shall designate in writing a representative in the Union.
texts.text: Where Article 3(2) applies, the controller or the processor shall designate in writing a representative in the Union.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The obligation laid down in paragraph 1 of this Article shall not apply to:
texts.text: The obligation laid down in paragraph 1 of this Article shall not apply to:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) processing which is occasional, does not include, on a large scale, processing of special categories of data as referred to in Article 9(1) or processing of personal data relating to criminal convictions and offences referred to in Article 10, and is unlikely to result in a risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, taking into account the nature, context, scope and purposes of the processing; or
texts.text: (a) processing which is occasional, does not include, on a large scale, processing of special categories of data as referred to in Article 9(1) or processing of personal data relating to criminal convictions and offences referred to in Article 10, and is unlikely to result in a risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, taking into account the nature, context, scope and purposes of the processing; or

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) a public authority or body.
texts.text: (b) a public authority or body.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The representative shall be established in one of the Member States where the data subjects, whose personal data are processed in relation to the offering of goods or services to them, or whose behaviour is monitored, are.
texts.text: The representative shall be established in one of the Member States where the data subjects, whose personal data are processed in relation to the offering of goods or services to them, or whose behaviour is monitored, are.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The representative shall be mandated by the controller or processor to be addressed in addition to or instead of the controller or the processor by, in particular, supervisory authorities and data subjects, on all issues related to processing, for the purposes of ensuring compliance with this Regulation.
texts.text: The representative shall be mandated by the controller or processor to be addressed in addition to or instead of the controller or the processor by, in particular, supervisory authorities and data subjects, on all issues related to processing, for the purposes of ensuring compliance with this Regulation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The designation of a representative by the controller or processor shall be without prejudice to legal actions which could be initiated against the controller or the processor themselves.
texts.text: The designation of a representative by the controller or processor shall be without prejudice to legal actions which could be initiated against the controller or the processor themselves.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Article 28
texts.text: Article 28

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Processor
texts.text: Processor

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where processing is to be carried out on behalf of a controller, the controller shall use only processors providing sufficient guarantees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that processing will meet the requirements of this Regulation and ensure the protection of the rights of the data subject.
texts.text: Where processing is to be carried out on behalf of a controller, the controller shall use only processors providing sufficient guarantees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that processing will meet the requirements of this Regulation and ensure the protection of the rights of the data subject.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The processor shall not engage another processor without prior specific or general written authorisation of the controller. In the case of general written authorisation, the processor shall inform the controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of other processors, thereby giving the controller the opportunity to object to such changes.
texts.text: The processor shall not engage another processor without prior specific or general written authorisation of the controller. In the case of general written authorisation, the processor shall inform the controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of other processors, thereby giving the controller the opportunity to object to such changes.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Processing by a processor shall be governed by a contract or other legal act under Union or Member State law, that is binding on the processor with regard to the controller and that sets out the subject-matter and duration of the processing, the nature and purpose of the processing, the type of personal data and categories of data subjects and the obligations and rights of the controller. That contract or other legal act shall stipulate, in particular, that the processor:
texts.text: Processing by a processor shall be governed by a contract or other legal act under Union or Member State law, that is binding on the processor with regard to the controller and that sets out the subject-matter and duration of the processing, the nature and purpose of the processing, the type of personal data and categories of data subjects and the obligations and rights of the controller. That contract or other legal act shall stipulate, in particular, that the processor:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) processes the personal data only on documented instructions from the controller, including with regard to transfers of personal data to a third country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the processor is subject; in such a case, the processor shall inform the controller of that legal requirement before processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;
texts.text: (a) processes the personal data only on documented instructions from the controller, including with regard to transfers of personal data to a third country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the processor is subject; in such a case, the processor shall inform the controller of that legal requirement before processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) ensures that persons authorised to process the personal data have committed themselves to confidentiality or are under an appropriate statutory obligation of confidentiality;
texts.text: (b) ensures that persons authorised to process the personal data have committed themselves to confidentiality or are under an appropriate statutory obligation of confidentiality;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) takes all measures required pursuant to Article 32;
texts.text: (c) takes all measures required pursuant to Article 32;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) respects the conditions referred to in paragraphs 2 and 4 for engaging another processor;
texts.text: (d) respects the conditions referred to in paragraphs 2 and 4 for engaging another processor;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) taking into account the nature of the processing, assists the controller by appropriate technical and organisational measures, insofar as this is possible, for the fulfilment of the controller's obligation to respond to requests for exercising the data subject's rights laid down in Chapter III;
texts.text: (e) taking into account the nature of the processing, assists the controller by appropriate technical and organisational measures, insofar as this is possible, for the fulfilment of the controller's obligation to respond to requests for exercising the data subject's rights laid down in Chapter III;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f) assists the controller in ensuring compliance with the obligations pursuant to Articles 32 to 36 taking into account the nature of processing and the information available to the processor;
texts.text: (f) assists the controller in ensuring compliance with the obligations pursuant to Articles 32 to 36 taking into account the nature of processing and the information available to the processor;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (g) at the choice of the controller, deletes or returns all the personal data to the controller after the end of the provision of services relating to processing, and deletes existing copies unless Union or Member State law requires storage of the personal data;
texts.text: (g) at the choice of the controller, deletes or returns all the personal data to the controller after the end of the provision of services relating to processing, and deletes existing copies unless Union or Member State law requires storage of the personal data;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (h) makes available to the controller all information necessary to demonstrate compliance with the obligations laid down in this Article and allow for and contribute to audits, including inspections, conducted by the controller or another auditor mandated by the controller.
texts.text: (h) makes available to the controller all information necessary to demonstrate compliance with the obligations laid down in this Article and allow for and contribute to audits, including inspections, conducted by the controller or another auditor mandated by the controller.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: With  regard  to  point  (h)  of  the  first  subparagraph,  the  processor  shall  immediately  inform  the  controller  if,  in  its opinion, an instruction infringes this Regulation or other Union or Member State data protection provisions.
texts.text: With  regard  to  point  (h)  of  the  first  subparagraph,  the  processor  shall  immediately  inform  the  controller  if,  in  its opinion, an instruction infringes this Regulation or other Union or Member State data protection provisions.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where a processor engages another processor for carrying out specific processing activities on behalf of the controller, the same data protection obligations as set out in the contract or other legal act between the controller and the processor as referred to in paragraph 3 shall be imposed on that other processor by way of a contract or other legal act under Union or Member State law, in particular providing sufficient guarantees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that the processing will meet the requirements of this Regulation. Where that other processor fails to fulfil its data protection obligations, the initial processor shall remain fully liable to the controller for the performance of that other processor's obligations.
texts.text: Where a processor engages another processor for carrying out specific processing activities on behalf of the controller, the same data protection obligations as set out in the contract or other legal act between the controller and the processor as referred to in paragraph 3 shall be imposed on that other processor by way of a contract or other legal act under Union or Member State law, in particular providing sufficient guarantees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that the processing will meet the requirements of this Regulation. Where that other processor fails to fulfil its data protection obligations, the initial processor shall remain fully liable to the controller for the performance of that other processor's obligations.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Adherence of a processor to an approved code of conduct as referred to in Article 40 or an approved certification mechanism as referred to in Article 42 may be used as an element by which to demonstrate sufficient guarantees as referred to in paragraphs 1 and 4 of this Article.
texts.text: Adherence of a processor to an approved code of conduct as referred to in Article 40 or an approved certification mechanism as referred to in Article 42 may be used as an element by which to demonstrate sufficient guarantees as referred to in paragraphs 1 and 4 of this Article.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Without prejudice to an individual contract between the controller and the processor, the contract or the other legal act referred to in paragraphs 3 and 4 of this Article may be based, in whole or in part, on standard contractual clauses referred to in paragraphs 7 and 8 of this Article, including when they are part of a certification granted to the controller or processor pursuant to Articles 42 and 43.
texts.text: Without prejudice to an individual contract between the controller and the processor, the contract or the other legal act referred to in paragraphs 3 and 4 of this Article may be based, in whole or in part, on standard contractual clauses referred to in paragraphs 7 and 8 of this Article, including when they are part of a certification granted to the controller or processor pursuant to Articles 42 and 43.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Commission may lay down standard contractual clauses for the matters referred to in paragraph 3 and 4 of this Article and in accordance with the examination procedure referred to in Article 93(2).
texts.text: The Commission may lay down standard contractual clauses for the matters referred to in paragraph 3 and 4 of this Article and in accordance with the examination procedure referred to in Article 93(2).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A supervisory authority may adopt standard contractual clauses for the matters referred to in paragraph 3 and 4 of this Article and in accordance with the consistency mechanism referred to in Article 63.
texts.text: A supervisory authority may adopt standard contractual clauses for the matters referred to in paragraph 3 and 4 of this Article and in accordance with the consistency mechanism referred to in Article 63.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The contract or the other legal act referred to in paragraphs 3 and 4 shall be in writing, including in electronic form.
texts.text: The contract or the other legal act referred to in paragraphs 3 and 4 shall be in writing, including in electronic form.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Without prejudice to Articles 82, 83 and 84, if a processor infringes this Regulation by determining the purposes and means of processing, the processor shall be considered to be a controller in respect of that processing.
texts.text: Without prejudice to Articles 82, 83 and 84, if a processor infringes this Regulation by determining the purposes and means of processing, the processor shall be considered to be a controller in respect of that processing.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 29
texts.text: Article 29

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Processing under the authority of the controller or processor
texts.text: Processing under the authority of the controller or processor

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  processor  and  any  person  acting  under  the  authority  of  the  controller  or  of  the  processor,  who  has  access  to personal data, shall not process those data except on instructions from the controller, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law.
texts.text: The  processor  and  any  person  acting  under  the  authority  of  the  controller  or  of  the  processor,  who  has  access  to personal data, shall not process those data except on instructions from the controller, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 30
texts.text: Article 30

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Records of processing activities
texts.text: Records of processing activities

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Each controller and, where applicable, the controller's representative, shall maintain a record of processing activities under its responsibility. That record shall contain all of the following information:
texts.text: Each controller and, where applicable, the controller's representative, shall maintain a record of processing activities under its responsibility. That record shall contain all of the following information:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) the name and contact details of the controller and, where applicable, the joint controller, the controller's representa­ tive and the data protection officer;
texts.text: (a) the name and contact details of the controller and, where applicable, the joint controller, the controller's representa tive and the data protection officer;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) the purposes of the processing;
texts.text: (b) the purposes of the processing;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) a description of the categories of data subjects and of the categories of personal data;
texts.text: (c) a description of the categories of data subjects and of the categories of personal data;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) the categories of recipients to whom the personal data have been or will be disclosed including recipients in third countries or international organisations;
texts.text: (d) the categories of recipients to whom the personal data have been or will be disclosed including recipients in third countries or international organisations;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) where applicable, transfers of personal data to a third country or an international organisation, including the identifi­ cation of that third country or international organisation and, in the case of transfers referred to in the second subparagraph of Article 49(1), the documentation of suitable safeguards;
texts.text: (e) where applicable, transfers of personal data to a third country or an international organisation, including the identifi cation of that third country or international organisation and, in the case of transfers referred to in the second subparagraph of Article 49(1), the documentation of suitable safeguards;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f) where possible, the envisaged time limits for erasure of the different categories of data;
texts.text: (f) where possible, the envisaged time limits for erasure of the different categories of data;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (g) where possible, a general description of the technical and organisational security measures referred to in Article 32(1).
texts.text: (g) where possible, a general description of the technical and organisational security measures referred to in Article 32(1).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Each processor and, where applicable, the processor's representative shall maintain a record of all categories of processing activities carried out on behalf of a controller, containing:
texts.text: Each processor and, where applicable, the processor's representative shall maintain a record of all categories of processing activities carried out on behalf of a controller, containing:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) the name and contact details of the processor or processors and of each controller on behalf of which the processor is acting, and, where applicable, of the controller's or the processor's representative, and the data protection officer;
texts.text: (a) the name and contact details of the processor or processors and of each controller on behalf of which the processor is acting, and, where applicable, of the controller's or the processor's representative, and the data protection officer;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) the categories of processing carried out on behalf of each controller;
texts.text: (b) the categories of processing carried out on behalf of each controller;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) where applicable, transfers of personal data to a third country or an international organisation, including the identifi­ cation of that third country or international organisation and, in the case of transfers referred to in the second subparagraph of Article 49(1), the documentation of suitable safeguards;
texts.text: (c) where applicable, transfers of personal data to a third country or an international organisation, including the identifi cation of that third country or international organisation and, in the case of transfers referred to in the second subparagraph of Article 49(1), the documentation of suitable safeguards;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) where possible, a general description of the technical and organisational security measures referred to in Article 32(1).
texts.text: (d) where possible, a general description of the technical and organisational security measures referred to in Article 32(1).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The records referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2 shall be in writing, including in electronic form.
texts.text: The records referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2 shall be in writing, including in electronic form.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The controller or the processor and, where applicable, the controller's or the processor's representative, shall make the record available to the supervisory authority on request.
texts.text: The controller or the processor and, where applicable, the controller's or the processor's representative, shall make the record available to the supervisory authority on request.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The obligations referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2 shall not apply to an enterprise or an organisation employing fewer than 250 persons unless the processing it carries out is likely to result in a risk to the rights and freedoms of data subjects, the processing is not occasional, or the processing includes special categories of data as referred to in Article 9(1) or personal data relating to criminal convictions and offences referred to in Article 10.
texts.text: The obligations referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2 shall not apply to an enterprise or an organisation employing fewer than 250 persons unless the processing it carries out is likely to result in a risk to the rights and freedoms of data subjects, the processing is not occasional, or the processing includes special categories of data as referred to in Article 9(1) or personal data relating to criminal convictions and offences referred to in Article 10.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 31
texts.text: Article 31

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Cooperation with the supervisory authority
texts.text: Cooperation with the supervisory authority

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  controller  and  the  processor  and,  where  applicable,  their  representatives,  shall  cooperate,  on  request,  with  the supervisory authority in the performance of its tasks.
texts.text: The  controller  and  the  processor  and,  where  applicable,  their  representatives,  shall  cooperate,  on  request,  with  the supervisory authority in the performance of its tasks.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Section 2
texts.text: Section 2

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Security of personal data
texts.text: Security of personal data

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 32
texts.text: Article 32

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Security of processing
texts.text: Security of processing

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller and the processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate:
texts.text: Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller and the processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) the pseudonymisation and encryption of personal data;
texts.text: (a) the pseudonymisation and encryption of personal data;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of processing systems and services;
texts.text: (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of processing systems and services;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) the ability to restore the availability and access to personal data in a timely manner in the event of a physical or technical incident;
texts.text: (c) the ability to restore the availability and access to personal data in a timely manner in the event of a physical or technical incident;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) a process for regularly testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the processing.
texts.text: (d) a process for regularly testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the processing.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In assessing the appropriate level of security account shall be taken in particular of the risks that are presented by processing, in particular from accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to personal data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed.
texts.text: In assessing the appropriate level of security account shall be taken in particular of the risks that are presented by processing, in particular from accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to personal data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Adherence to an approved code of conduct as referred to in Article 40 or an approved certification mechanism as referred to in Article 42 may be used as an element by which to demonstrate compliance with the requirements set out in paragraph 1 of this Article.
texts.text: Adherence to an approved code of conduct as referred to in Article 40 or an approved certification mechanism as referred to in Article 42 may be used as an element by which to demonstrate compliance with the requirements set out in paragraph 1 of this Article.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The controller and processor shall take steps to ensure that any natural person acting under the authority of the controller or the processor who has access to personal data does not process them except on instructions from the controller, unless he or she is required to do so by Union or Member State law.
texts.text: The controller and processor shall take steps to ensure that any natural person acting under the authority of the controller or the processor who has access to personal data does not process them except on instructions from the controller, unless he or she is required to do so by Union or Member State law.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 33
texts.text: Article 33

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Notification of a personal data breach to the supervisory authority
texts.text: Notification of a personal data breach to the supervisory authority

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In the case of a personal data breach, the controller shall without undue delay and, where feasible, not later than 72 hours after having become aware of it, notify the personal data breach to the supervisory authority competent in accordance with Article 55, unless the personal data breach is unlikely to result in a risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons. Where the notification to the supervisory authority is not made within 72 hours, it shall be accompanied by reasons for the delay.
texts.text: In the case of a personal data breach, the controller shall without undue delay and, where feasible, not later than 72 hours after having become aware of it, notify the personal data breach to the supervisory authority competent in accordance with Article 55, unless the personal data breach is unlikely to result in a risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons. Where the notification to the supervisory authority is not made within 72 hours, it shall be accompanied by reasons for the delay.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The processor shall notify the controller without undue delay after becoming aware of a personal data breach.
texts.text: The processor shall notify the controller without undue delay after becoming aware of a personal data breach.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The notification referred to in paragraph 1 shall at least:
texts.text: The notification referred to in paragraph 1 shall at least:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) describe the nature of the personal data breach including where possible, the categories and approximate number of data subjects concerned and the categories and approximate number of personal data records concerned;
texts.text: (a) describe the nature of the personal data breach including where possible, the categories and approximate number of data subjects concerned and the categories and approximate number of personal data records concerned;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) communicate the name and contact details of the data protection officer or other contact point where more information can be obtained;
texts.text: (b) communicate the name and contact details of the data protection officer or other contact point where more information can be obtained;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) describe the likely consequences of the personal data breach;
texts.text: (c) describe the likely consequences of the personal data breach;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) describe the measures taken or proposed to be taken by the controller to address the personal data breach, including, where appropriate, measures to mitigate its possible adverse effects.
texts.text: (d) describe the measures taken or proposed to be taken by the controller to address the personal data breach, including, where appropriate, measures to mitigate its possible adverse effects.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where, and in so far as, it is not possible to provide the information at the same time, the information may be provided in phases without undue further delay.
texts.text: Where, and in so far as, it is not possible to provide the information at the same time, the information may be provided in phases without undue further delay.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The controller shall document any personal data breaches, comprising the facts relating to the personal data breach, its effects and the remedial action taken. That documentation shall enable the supervisory authority to verify compliance with this Article.
texts.text: The controller shall document any personal data breaches, comprising the facts relating to the personal data breach, its effects and the remedial action taken. That documentation shall enable the supervisory authority to verify compliance with this Article.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 34
texts.text: Article 34

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Communication of a personal data breach to the data subject
texts.text: Communication of a personal data breach to the data subject

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: When the personal data breach is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller shall communicate the personal data breach to the data subject without undue delay.
texts.text: When the personal data breach is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller shall communicate the personal data breach to the data subject without undue delay.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The communication to the data subject referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article shall describe in clear and plain language the nature of the personal data breach and contain at least the information and measures referred to in points (b), (c) and (d) of Article 33(3).
texts.text: The communication to the data subject referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article shall describe in clear and plain language the nature of the personal data breach and contain at least the information and measures referred to in points (b), (c) and (d) of Article 33(3).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The communication to the data subject referred to in paragraph 1 shall not be required if any of the following conditions are met:
texts.text: The communication to the data subject referred to in paragraph 1 shall not be required if any of the following conditions are met:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) the controller has implemented appropriate technical and organisational protection measures, and those measures were applied to the personal data affected by the personal data breach, in particular those that render the personal data unintelligible to any person who is not authorised to access it, such as encryption;
texts.text: (a) the controller has implemented appropriate technical and organisational protection measures, and those measures were applied to the personal data affected by the personal data breach, in particular those that render the personal data unintelligible to any person who is not authorised to access it, such as encryption;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) the controller has taken subsequent measures which ensure that the high risk to the rights and freedoms of data subjects referred to in paragraph 1 is no longer likely to materialise;
texts.text: (b) the controller has taken subsequent measures which ensure that the high risk to the rights and freedoms of data subjects referred to in paragraph 1 is no longer likely to materialise;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) it would involve disproportionate effort. In such a case, there shall instead be a public communication or similar measure whereby the data subjects are informed in an equally effective manner.
texts.text: (c) it would involve disproportionate effort. In such a case, there shall instead be a public communication or similar measure whereby the data subjects are informed in an equally effective manner.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: If the controller has not already communicated the personal data breach to the data subject, the supervisory authority, having considered the likelihood of the personal data breach resulting in a high risk, may require it to do so or may decide that any of the conditions referred to in paragraph 3 are met.
texts.text: If the controller has not already communicated the personal data breach to the data subject, the supervisory authority, having considered the likelihood of the personal data breach resulting in a high risk, may require it to do so or may decide that any of the conditions referred to in paragraph 3 are met.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Section 3
texts.text: Section 3

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Data protection impact assessment and prior consultation
texts.text: Data protection impact assessment and prior consultation

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 35
texts.text: Article 35

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Data protection impact assessment
texts.text: Data protection impact assessment

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where a type of processing in particular using new technologies, and taking into account the nature, scope, context and purposes of the processing, is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller shall, prior to the processing, carry out an assessment of the impact of the envisaged processing operations on the protection of personal data. A single assessment may address a set of similar processing operations that present similar high risks.
texts.text: Where a type of processing in particular using new technologies, and taking into account the nature, scope, context and purposes of the processing, is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller shall, prior to the processing, carry out an assessment of the impact of the envisaged processing operations on the protection of personal data. A single assessment may address a set of similar processing operations that present similar high risks.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The controller shall seek the advice of the data protection officer, where designated, when carrying out a data protection impact assessment.
texts.text: The controller shall seek the advice of the data protection officer, where designated, when carrying out a data protection impact assessment.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A data protection impact assessment referred to in paragraph 1 shall in particular be required in the case of:
texts.text: A data protection impact assessment referred to in paragraph 1 shall in particular be required in the case of:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) a systematic and extensive evaluation of personal aspects relating to natural persons which is based on automated processing, including profiling, and on which decisions are based that produce legal effects concerning the natural person or similarly significantly affect the natural person;
texts.text: (a) a systematic and extensive evaluation of personal aspects relating to natural persons which is based on automated processing, including profiling, and on which decisions are based that produce legal effects concerning the natural person or similarly significantly affect the natural person;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) processing on a large scale of special categories of data referred to in Article 9(1), or of personal data relating to criminal convictions and offences referred to in Article 10; or
texts.text: (b) processing on a large scale of special categories of data referred to in Article 9(1), or of personal data relating to criminal convictions and offences referred to in Article 10; or

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) a systematic monitoring of a publicly accessible area on a large scale.
texts.text: (c) a systematic monitoring of a publicly accessible area on a large scale.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The supervisory authority shall establish and make public a list of the kind of processing operations which are subject to the requirement for a data protection impact assessment pursuant to paragraph 1. The supervisory authority shall communicate those lists to the Board referred to in Article 68.
texts.text: The supervisory authority shall establish and make public a list of the kind of processing operations which are subject to the requirement for a data protection impact assessment pursuant to paragraph 1. The supervisory authority shall communicate those lists to the Board referred to in Article 68.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The supervisory authority may also establish and make public a list of the kind of processing operations for which no data protection impact assessment is required. The supervisory authority shall communicate those lists to the Board.
