texts.orig: (f) the acceptance by the controller or processor established on the territory of a Member State of liability for any breaches of the binding corporate rules by any member concerned not established in the Union; the controller or the processor shall be exempt from that liability, in whole or in part, only if it proves that that member is not responsible for the event giving rise to the damage;
texts.text: (f) the acceptance by the controller or processor established on the territory of a Member State of liability for any breaches of the binding corporate rules by any member concerned not established in the Union; the controller or the processor shall be exempt from that liability, in whole or in part, only if it proves that that member is not responsible for the event giving rise to the damage;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (g) how the information on the binding corporate rules, in particular on the provisions referred to in points (d), (e) and (f) of this paragraph is provided to the data subjects in addition to Articles 13 and 14;
texts.text: (g) how the information on the binding corporate rules, in particular on the provisions referred to in points (d), (e) and (f) of this paragraph is provided to the data subjects in addition to Articles 13 and 14;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (h) the tasks of any data protection officer designated in accordance with Article 37 or any other person or entity in charge of the monitoring compliance with the binding corporate rules within the group of undertakings, or group of enterprises engaged in a joint economic activity, as well as monitoring training and complaint-handling;
texts.text: (h) the tasks of any data protection officer designated in accordance with Article 37 or any other person or entity in charge of the monitoring compliance with the binding corporate rules within the group of undertakings, or group of enterprises engaged in a joint economic activity, as well as monitoring training and complaint-handling;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (i) the complaint procedures;
texts.text: (i) the complaint procedures;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (j) the mechanisms within the group of undertakings, or group of enterprises engaged in a joint economic activity for ensuring the verification of compliance with the binding corporate rules. Such mechanisms shall include data protection audits and methods for ensuring corrective actions to protect the rights of the data subject. Results of such verification should be communicated to the person or entity referred to in point (h) and to the board of the controlling undertaking of a group of undertakings, or of the group of enterprises engaged in a joint economic activity, and should be available upon request to the competent supervisory authority;
texts.text: (j) the mechanisms within the group of undertakings, or group of enterprises engaged in a joint economic activity for ensuring the verification of compliance with the binding corporate rules. Such mechanisms shall include data protection audits and methods for ensuring corrective actions to protect the rights of the data subject. Results of such verification should be communicated to the person or entity referred to in point (h) and to the board of the controlling undertaking of a group of undertakings, or of the group of enterprises engaged in a joint economic activity, and should be available upon request to the competent supervisory authority;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (k) the mechanisms for reporting and recording changes to the rules and reporting those changes to the supervisory authority;
texts.text: (k) the mechanisms for reporting and recording changes to the rules and reporting those changes to the supervisory authority;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (l) the cooperation mechanism with the supervisory authority to ensure compliance by any member of the group of undertakings, or group of enterprises engaged in a joint economic activity, in particular by making available to the supervisory authority the results of verifications of the measures referred to in point (j);
texts.text: (l) the cooperation mechanism with the supervisory authority to ensure compliance by any member of the group of undertakings, or group of enterprises engaged in a joint economic activity, in particular by making available to the supervisory authority the results of verifications of the measures referred to in point (j);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (m) the mechanisms for reporting to the competent supervisory authority any legal requirements to which a member of the group of undertakings, or group of enterprises engaged in a joint economic activity is subject in a third country which are likely to have a substantial adverse effect on the guarantees provided by the binding corporate rules; and
texts.text: (m) the mechanisms for reporting to the competent supervisory authority any legal requirements to which a member of the group of undertakings, or group of enterprises engaged in a joint economic activity is subject in a third country which are likely to have a substantial adverse effect on the guarantees provided by the binding corporate rules; and

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (n) the appropriate data protection training to personnel having permanent or regular access to personal data.
texts.text: (n) the appropriate data protection training to personnel having permanent or regular access to personal data.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Commission may specify the format and procedures for the exchange of information between controllers, processors and supervisory authorities for binding corporate rules within the meaning of this Article. Those implementing acts shall be adopted in accordance with the examination procedure set out in Article 93(2).
texts.text: The Commission may specify the format and procedures for the exchange of information between controllers, processors and supervisory authorities for binding corporate rules within the meaning of this Article. Those implementing acts shall be adopted in accordance with the examination procedure set out in Article 93(2).

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 48
texts.text: Article 48

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Transfers or disclosures not authorised by Union law
texts.text: Transfers or disclosures not authorised by Union law

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Any  judgment  of  a  court  or  tribunal  and  any  decision  of  an  administrative  authority  of  a  third  country  requiring  a controller  or  processor  to  transfer  or  disclose  personal  data  may  only  be  recognised  or  enforceable  in  any  manner  if based  on  an  international  agreement,  such  as  a  mutual  legal  assistance  treaty,  in  force  between  the  requesting  third country and the Union or a Member State, without prejudice to other grounds for transfer pursuant to this Chapter.
texts.text: Any  judgment  of  a  court  or  tribunal  and  any  decision  of  an  administrative  authority  of  a  third  country  requiring  a controller  or  processor  to  transfer  or  disclose  personal  data  may  only  be  recognised  or  enforceable  in  any  manner  if based  on  an  international  agreement,  such  as  a  mutual  legal  assistance  treaty,  in  force  between  the  requesting  third country and the Union or a Member State, without prejudice to other grounds for transfer pursuant to this Chapter.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 49
texts.text: Article 49

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Derogations for specific situations
texts.text: Derogations for specific situations

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In the absence of an adequacy decision pursuant to Article 45(3), or of appropriate safeguards pursuant to Article 46, including binding corporate rules, a transfer or a set of transfers of personal data to a third country or an in­ ternational organisation shall take place only on one of the following conditions:
texts.text: In the absence of an adequacy decision pursuant to Article 45(3), or of appropriate safeguards pursuant to Article 46, including binding corporate rules, a transfer or a set of transfers of personal data to a third country or an in ternational organisation shall take place only on one of the following conditions:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) the data subject has explicitly consented to the proposed transfer, after having been informed of the possible risks of such transfers for the data subject due to the absence of an adequacy decision and appropriate safeguards;
texts.text: (a) the data subject has explicitly consented to the proposed transfer, after having been informed of the possible risks of such transfers for the data subject due to the absence of an adequacy decision and appropriate safeguards;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) the transfer is necessary for the performance of a contract between the data subject and the controller or the implementation of pre-contractual measures taken at the data subject's request;
texts.text: (b) the transfer is necessary for the performance of a contract between the data subject and the controller or the implementation of pre-contractual measures taken at the data subject's request;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) the transfer is necessary for the conclusion or performance of a contract concluded in the interest of the data subject between the controller and another natural or legal person;
texts.text: (c) the transfer is necessary for the conclusion or performance of a contract concluded in the interest of the data subject between the controller and another natural or legal person;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) the transfer is necessary for important reasons of public interest;
texts.text: (d) the transfer is necessary for important reasons of public interest;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) the transfer is necessary for the establishment, exercise or defence of legal claims;
texts.text: (e) the transfer is necessary for the establishment, exercise or defence of legal claims;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f) the transfer is necessary in order to protect the vital interests of the data subject or of other persons, where the data subject is physically or legally incapable of giving consent;
texts.text: (f) the transfer is necessary in order to protect the vital interests of the data subject or of other persons, where the data subject is physically or legally incapable of giving consent;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (g) the transfer is made from a register which according to Union or Member State law is intended to provide information to the public and which is open to consultation either by the public in general or by any person who can demonstrate a legitimate interest, but only to the extent that the conditions laid down by Union or Member State law for consultation are fulfilled in the particular case.
texts.text: (g) the transfer is made from a register which according to Union or Member State law is intended to provide information to the public and which is open to consultation either by the public in general or by any person who can demonstrate a legitimate interest, but only to the extent that the conditions laid down by Union or Member State law for consultation are fulfilled in the particular case.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Where a transfer  could  not  be  based  on  a  provision  in  Article  45  or  46,  including  the  provisions  on  binding  corporate rules,  and  none  of  the  derogations  for  a  specific  situation  referred  to  in  the  first  subparagraph  of  this  paragraph  is applicable,  a  transfer  to  a  third  country  or  an  international  organisation  may  take  place  only  if  the  transfer  is  not repetitive,  concerns  only  a  limited  number  of  data  subjects,  is  necessary  for  the  purposes  of  compelling  legitimate interests  pursued  by  the  controller  which  are  not  overridden  by  the  interests  or  rights  and  freedoms  of  the  data  subject, and  the  controller  has  assessed  all  the  circumstances  surrounding  the  data  transfer  and  has  on  the  basis  of  that assessment  provided  suitable  safeguards  with  regard  to  the  protection  of  personal  data.  The  controller  shall  inform  the supervisory  authority  of  the  transfer.  The  controller  shall,  in  addition  to  providing  the  information  referred  to  in Articles 13 and 14, inform the data subject of the transfer and on the compelling legitimate interests pursued.
texts.text: Where a transfer  could  not  be  based  on  a  provision  in  Article  45  or  46,  including  the  provisions  on  binding  corporate rules,  and  none  of  the  derogations  for  a  specific  situation  referred  to  in  the  first  subparagraph  of  this  paragraph  is applicable,  a  transfer  to  a  third  country  or  an  international  organisation  may  take  place  only  if  the  transfer  is  not repetitive,  concerns  only  a  limited  number  of  data  subjects,  is  necessary  for  the  purposes  of  compelling  legitimate interests  pursued  by  the  controller  which  are  not  overridden  by  the  interests  or  rights  and  freedoms  of  the  data  subject, and  the  controller  has  assessed  all  the  circumstances  surrounding  the  data  transfer  and  has  on  the  basis  of  that assessment  provided  suitable  safeguards  with  regard  to  the  protection  of  personal  data.  The  controller  shall  inform  the supervisory  authority  of  the  transfer.  The  controller  shall,  in  addition  to  providing  the  information  referred  to  in Articles 13 and 14, inform the data subject of the transfer and on the compelling legitimate interests pursued.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A transfer pursuant to point (g) of the first subparagraph of paragraph 1 shall not involve the entirety of the personal data or entire categories of the personal data contained in the register. Where the register is intended for consultation by persons having a legitimate interest, the transfer shall be made only at the request of those persons or if they are to be the recipients.
texts.text: A transfer pursuant to point (g) of the first subparagraph of paragraph 1 shall not involve the entirety of the personal data or entire categories of the personal data contained in the register. Where the register is intended for consultation by persons having a legitimate interest, the transfer shall be made only at the request of those persons or if they are to be the recipients.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Points (a), (b) and (c) of the first subparagraph of paragraph 1 and the second subparagraph thereof shall not apply to activities carried out by public authorities in the exercise of their public powers.
texts.text: Points (a), (b) and (c) of the first subparagraph of paragraph 1 and the second subparagraph thereof shall not apply to activities carried out by public authorities in the exercise of their public powers.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The public interest referred to in point (d) of the first subparagraph of paragraph 1 shall be recognised in Union law or in the law of the Member State to which the controller is subject.
texts.text: The public interest referred to in point (d) of the first subparagraph of paragraph 1 shall be recognised in Union law or in the law of the Member State to which the controller is subject.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In the absence of an adequacy decision, Union or Member State law may, for important reasons of public interest, expressly set limits to the transfer of specific categories of personal data to a third country or an international organisation. Member States shall notify such provisions to the Commission.
texts.text: In the absence of an adequacy decision, Union or Member State law may, for important reasons of public interest, expressly set limits to the transfer of specific categories of personal data to a third country or an international organisation. Member States shall notify such provisions to the Commission.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The controller or processor shall document the assessment as well as the suitable safeguards referred to in the second subparagraph of paragraph 1 of this Article in the records referred to in Article 30.
texts.text: The controller or processor shall document the assessment as well as the suitable safeguards referred to in the second subparagraph of paragraph 1 of this Article in the records referred to in Article 30.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 50
texts.text: Article 50

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: International cooperation for the protection of personal data
texts.text: International cooperation for the protection of personal data

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In  relation  to  third  countries  and  international  organisations,  the  Commission  and  supervisory  authorities  shall  take appropriate steps to:
texts.text: In  relation  to  third  countries  and  international  organisations,  the  Commission  and  supervisory  authorities  shall  take appropriate steps to:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) develop international cooperation mechanisms to facilitate the effective enforcement of legislation for the protection of personal data;
texts.text: (a) develop international cooperation mechanisms to facilitate the effective enforcement of legislation for the protection of personal data;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) provide international mutual assistance in the enforcement of legislation for the protection of personal data, including through notification, complaint referral, investigative assistance and information exchange, subject to appropriate safeguards for the protection of personal data and other fundamental rights and freedoms;
texts.text: (b) provide international mutual assistance in the enforcement of legislation for the protection of personal data, including through notification, complaint referral, investigative assistance and information exchange, subject to appropriate safeguards for the protection of personal data and other fundamental rights and freedoms;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) engage relevant stakeholders in discussion and activities aimed at furthering international cooperation in the enforcement of legislation for the protection of personal data;
texts.text: (c) engage relevant stakeholders in discussion and activities aimed at furthering international cooperation in the enforcement of legislation for the protection of personal data;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) promote the exchange and documentation of personal data protection legislation and practice, including on jurisdic­ tional conflicts with third countries.
texts.text: (d) promote the exchange and documentation of personal data protection legislation and practice, including on jurisdic tional conflicts with third countries.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: CHAPTER VI
texts.text: CHAPTER VI

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Independent supervisory authorities
texts.text: Independent supervisory authorities

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Section 1
texts.text: Section 1

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Independent status
texts.text: Independent status

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 51
texts.text: Article 51

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Supervisory authority
texts.text: Supervisory authority

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Each Member State shall provide for one or more independent public authorities to be responsible for monitoring the application of this Regulation, in order to protect the fundamental rights and freedoms of natural persons in relation to processing and to facilitate the free flow of personal data within the Union ('supervisory authority').
texts.text: Each Member State shall provide for one or more independent public authorities to be responsible for monitoring the application of this Regulation, in order to protect the fundamental rights and freedoms of natural persons in relation to processing and to facilitate the free flow of personal data within the Union ('supervisory authority').

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Each supervisory authority shall contribute to the consistent application of this Regulation throughout the Union. For that purpose, the supervisory authorities shall cooperate with each other and the Commission in accordance with Chapter VII.
texts.text: Each supervisory authority shall contribute to the consistent application of this Regulation throughout the Union. For that purpose, the supervisory authorities shall cooperate with each other and the Commission in accordance with Chapter VII.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where more than one supervisory authority is established in a Member State, that Member State shall designate the supervisory authority which is to represent those authorities in the Board and shall set out the mechanism to ensure compliance by the other authorities with the rules relating to the consistency mechanism referred to in Article 63.
texts.text: Where more than one supervisory authority is established in a Member State, that Member State shall designate the supervisory authority which is to represent those authorities in the Board and shall set out the mechanism to ensure compliance by the other authorities with the rules relating to the consistency mechanism referred to in Article 63.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Each Member State shall notify to the Commission the provisions of its law which it adopts pursuant to this Chapter, by 25 May 2018 and, without delay, any subsequent amendment affecting them.
texts.text: Each Member State shall notify to the Commission the provisions of its law which it adopts pursuant to this Chapter, by 25 May 2018 and, without delay, any subsequent amendment affecting them.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 52
texts.text: Article 52

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Independence
texts.text: Independence

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Each supervisory authority shall act with complete independence in performing its tasks and exercising its powers in accordance with this Regulation.
texts.text: Each supervisory authority shall act with complete independence in performing its tasks and exercising its powers in accordance with this Regulation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The member or members of each supervisory authority shall, in the performance of their tasks and exercise of their powers in accordance with this Regulation, remain free from external influence, whether direct or indirect, and shall neither seek nor take instructions from anybody.
texts.text: The member or members of each supervisory authority shall, in the performance of their tasks and exercise of their powers in accordance with this Regulation, remain free from external influence, whether direct or indirect, and shall neither seek nor take instructions from anybody.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Member or members of each supervisory authority shall refrain from any action incompatible with their duties and shall not, during their term of office, engage in any incompatible occupation, whether gainful or not.
texts.text: Member or members of each supervisory authority shall refrain from any action incompatible with their duties and shall not, during their term of office, engage in any incompatible occupation, whether gainful or not.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Each Member State shall ensure that each supervisory authority is provided with the human, technical and financial resources, premises and infrastructure necessary for the effective performance of its tasks and exercise of its powers, including those to be carried out in the context of mutual assistance, cooperation and participation in the Board.
texts.text: Each Member State shall ensure that each supervisory authority is provided with the human, technical and financial resources, premises and infrastructure necessary for the effective performance of its tasks and exercise of its powers, including those to be carried out in the context of mutual assistance, cooperation and participation in the Board.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Each Member State shall ensure that each supervisory authority chooses and has its own staff which shall be subject to the exclusive direction of the member or members of the supervisory authority concerned.
texts.text: Each Member State shall ensure that each supervisory authority chooses and has its own staff which shall be subject to the exclusive direction of the member or members of the supervisory authority concerned.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Each Member State shall ensure that each supervisory authority is subject to financial control which does not affect its independence and that it has separate, public annual budgets, which may be part of the overall state or national budget.
texts.text: Each Member State shall ensure that each supervisory authority is subject to financial control which does not affect its independence and that it has separate, public annual budgets, which may be part of the overall state or national budget.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 53
texts.text: Article 53

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: General conditions for the members of the supervisory authority
texts.text: General conditions for the members of the supervisory authority

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Member States shall provide for each member of their supervisory authorities to be appointed by means of a transparent procedure by:
texts.text: Member States shall provide for each member of their supervisory authorities to be appointed by means of a transparent procedure by:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: their parliament;
texts.text: their parliament;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: their government;
texts.text: their government;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: their head of State; or
texts.text: their head of State; or

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: an independent body entrusted with the appointment under Member State law.
texts.text: an independent body entrusted with the appointment under Member State law.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Each member shall have the qualifications, experience and skills, in particular in the area of the protection of personal data, required to perform its duties and exercise its powers.
texts.text: Each member shall have the qualifications, experience and skills, in particular in the area of the protection of personal data, required to perform its duties and exercise its powers.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The duties of a member shall end in the event of the expiry of the term of office, resignation or compulsory retirement, in accordance with the law of the Member State concerned.
texts.text: The duties of a member shall end in the event of the expiry of the term of office, resignation or compulsory retirement, in accordance with the law of the Member State concerned.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A member shall be dismissed only in cases of serious misconduct or if the member no longer fulfils the conditions required for the performance of the duties.
texts.text: A member shall be dismissed only in cases of serious misconduct or if the member no longer fulfils the conditions required for the performance of the duties.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 54
texts.text: Article 54

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rules on the establishment of the supervisory authority
texts.text: Rules on the establishment of the supervisory authority

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Each Member State shall provide by law for all of the following:
texts.text: Each Member State shall provide by law for all of the following:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) the establishment of each supervisory authority;
texts.text: (a) the establishment of each supervisory authority;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) the qualifications and eligibility conditions required to be appointed as member of each supervisory authority;
texts.text: (b) the qualifications and eligibility conditions required to be appointed as member of each supervisory authority;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) the rules and procedures for the appointment of the member or members of each supervisory authority;
texts.text: (c) the rules and procedures for the appointment of the member or members of each supervisory authority;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) the duration of the term of the member or members of each supervisory authority of no less than four years, except for the first appointment after 24 May 2016, part of which may take place for a shorter period where that is necessary to protect the independence of the supervisory authority by means of a staggered appointment procedure;
texts.text: (d) the duration of the term of the member or members of each supervisory authority of no less than four years, except for the first appointment after 24 May 2016, part of which may take place for a shorter period where that is necessary to protect the independence of the supervisory authority by means of a staggered appointment procedure;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) whether and, if so, for how many terms the member or members of each supervisory authority is eligible for reappointment;
texts.text: (e) whether and, if so, for how many terms the member or members of each supervisory authority is eligible for reappointment;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f) the conditions governing the obligations of the member or members and staff of each supervisory authority, prohibitions on actions, occupations and benefits incompatible therewith during and after the term of office and rules governing the cessation of employment.
texts.text: (f) the conditions governing the obligations of the member or members and staff of each supervisory authority, prohibitions on actions, occupations and benefits incompatible therewith during and after the term of office and rules governing the cessation of employment.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The member or members and the staff of each supervisory authority shall, in accordance with Union or Member State law, be subject to a duty of professional secrecy both during and after their term of office, with regard to any confidential information which has come to their knowledge in the course of the performance of their tasks or exercise of their powers. During their term of office, that duty of professional secrecy shall in particular apply to reporting by natural persons of infringements of this Regulation.
texts.text: The member or members and the staff of each supervisory authority shall, in accordance with Union or Member State law, be subject to a duty of professional secrecy both during and after their term of office, with regard to any confidential information which has come to their knowledge in the course of the performance of their tasks or exercise of their powers. During their term of office, that duty of professional secrecy shall in particular apply to reporting by natural persons of infringements of this Regulation.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Section 2
texts.text: Section 2

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Competence, tasks and powers
texts.text: Competence, tasks and powers

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 55
texts.text: Article 55

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Competence
texts.text: Competence

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Each supervisory authority shall be competent for the performance of the tasks assigned to and the exercise of the powers conferred on it in accordance with this Regulation on the territory of its own Member State.
texts.text: Each supervisory authority shall be competent for the performance of the tasks assigned to and the exercise of the powers conferred on it in accordance with this Regulation on the territory of its own Member State.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where processing is carried out by public authorities or private bodies acting on the basis of point (c) or (e) of Article 6(1), the supervisory authority of the Member State concerned shall be competent. In such cases Article 56 does not apply.
texts.text: Where processing is carried out by public authorities or private bodies acting on the basis of point (c) or (e) of Article 6(1), the supervisory authority of the Member State concerned shall be competent. In such cases Article 56 does not apply.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Supervisory authorities shall not be competent to supervise processing operations of courts acting in their judicial capacity.
texts.text: Supervisory authorities shall not be competent to supervise processing operations of courts acting in their judicial capacity.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 56
texts.text: Article 56

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Competence of the lead supervisory authority
texts.text: Competence of the lead supervisory authority

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Without prejudice to Article 55, the supervisory authority of the main establishment or of the single establishment of the controller or processor shall be competent to act as lead supervisory authority for the cross-border processing carried out by that controller or processor in accordance with the procedure provided in Article 60.
texts.text: Without prejudice to Article 55, the supervisory authority of the main establishment or of the single establishment of the controller or processor shall be competent to act as lead supervisory authority for the cross-border processing carried out by that controller or processor in accordance with the procedure provided in Article 60.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: By derogation from paragraph 1, each supervisory authority shall be competent to handle a complaint lodged with it or a possible infringement of this Regulation, if the subject matter relates only to an establishment in its Member State or substantially affects data subjects only in its Member State.
texts.text: By derogation from paragraph 1, each supervisory authority shall be competent to handle a complaint lodged with it or a possible infringement of this Regulation, if the subject matter relates only to an establishment in its Member State or substantially affects data subjects only in its Member State.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In the cases referred to in paragraph 2 of this Article, the supervisory authority shall inform the lead supervisory authority without delay on that matter. Within a period of three weeks after being informed the lead supervisory authority shall decide whether or not it will handle the case in accordance with the procedure provided in Article 60, taking into account whether or not there is an establishment of the controller or processor in the Member State of which the supervisory authority informed it.
texts.text: In the cases referred to in paragraph 2 of this Article, the supervisory authority shall inform the lead supervisory authority without delay on that matter. Within a period of three weeks after being informed the lead supervisory authority shall decide whether or not it will handle the case in accordance with the procedure provided in Article 60, taking into account whether or not there is an establishment of the controller or processor in the Member State of which the supervisory authority informed it.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where the lead supervisory authority decides to handle the case, the procedure provided in Article 60 shall apply. The supervisory authority which informed the lead supervisory authority may submit to the lead supervisory authority a draft for a decision. The lead supervisory authority shall take utmost account of that draft when preparing the draft decision referred to in Article 60(3).
texts.text: Where the lead supervisory authority decides to handle the case, the procedure provided in Article 60 shall apply. The supervisory authority which informed the lead supervisory authority may submit to the lead supervisory authority a draft for a decision. The lead supervisory authority shall take utmost account of that draft when preparing the draft decision referred to in Article 60(3).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where the lead supervisory authority decides not to handle the case, the supervisory authority which informed the lead supervisory authority shall handle it according to Articles 61 and 62.
texts.text: Where the lead supervisory authority decides not to handle the case, the supervisory authority which informed the lead supervisory authority shall handle it according to Articles 61 and 62.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The lead supervisory authority shall be the sole interlocutor of the controller or processor for the cross-border processing carried out by that controller or processor.
texts.text: The lead supervisory authority shall be the sole interlocutor of the controller or processor for the cross-border processing carried out by that controller or processor.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 57
texts.text: Article 57

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Tasks
texts.text: Tasks

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Without prejudice to other tasks set out under this Regulation, each supervisory authority shall on its territory:
texts.text: Without prejudice to other tasks set out under this Regulation, each supervisory authority shall on its territory:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) monitor and enforce the application of this Regulation;
texts.text: (a) monitor and enforce the application of this Regulation;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) promote public awareness and understanding of the risks, rules, safeguards and rights in relation to processing. Activities addressed specifically to children shall receive specific attention;
texts.text: (b) promote public awareness and understanding of the risks, rules, safeguards and rights in relation to processing. Activities addressed specifically to children shall receive specific attention;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) advise, in accordance with Member State law, the national parliament, the government, and other institutions and bodies on legislative and administrative measures relating to the protection of natural persons' rights and freedoms with regard to processing;
texts.text: (c) advise, in accordance with Member State law, the national parliament, the government, and other institutions and bodies on legislative and administrative measures relating to the protection of natural persons' rights and freedoms with regard to processing;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) promote the awareness of controllers and processors of their obligations under this Regulation;
texts.text: (d) promote the awareness of controllers and processors of their obligations under this Regulation;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) upon request, provide information to any data subject concerning the exercise of their rights under this Regulation and, if appropriate, cooperate with the supervisory authorities in other Member States to that end;
texts.text: (e) upon request, provide information to any data subject concerning the exercise of their rights under this Regulation and, if appropriate, cooperate with the supervisory authorities in other Member States to that end;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f) handle complaints lodged by a data subject, or by a body, organisation or association in accordance with Article 80, and investigate, to the extent appropriate, the subject matter of the complaint and inform the complainant of the progress and the outcome of the investigation within a reasonable period, in particular if further investigation or coordination with another supervisory authority is necessary;
texts.text: (f) handle complaints lodged by a data subject, or by a body, organisation or association in accordance with Article 80, and investigate, to the extent appropriate, the subject matter of the complaint and inform the complainant of the progress and the outcome of the investigation within a reasonable period, in particular if further investigation or coordination with another supervisory authority is necessary;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (g) cooperate with, including sharing information and provide mutual assistance to, other supervisory authorities with a view to ensuring the consistency of application and enforcement of this Regulation;
texts.text: (g) cooperate with, including sharing information and provide mutual assistance to, other supervisory authorities with a view to ensuring the consistency of application and enforcement of this Regulation;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (h) conduct investigations on the application of this Regulation, including on the basis of information received from another supervisory authority or other public authority;
texts.text: (h) conduct investigations on the application of this Regulation, including on the basis of information received from another supervisory authority or other public authority;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (i) monitor relevant developments, insofar as they have an impact on the protection of personal data, in particular the development of information and communication technologies and commercial practices;
texts.text: (i) monitor relevant developments, insofar as they have an impact on the protection of personal data, in particular the development of information and communication technologies and commercial practices;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (j) adopt standard contractual clauses referred to in Article 28(8) and in point (d) of Article 46(2);
texts.text: (j) adopt standard contractual clauses referred to in Article 28(8) and in point (d) of Article 46(2);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (k) establish and maintain a list in relation to the requirement for data protection impact assessment pursuant to Article 35(4);
texts.text: (k) establish and maintain a list in relation to the requirement for data protection impact assessment pursuant to Article 35(4);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (l) give advice on the processing operations referred to in Article 36(2);
texts.text: (l) give advice on the processing operations referred to in Article 36(2);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (m) encourage the drawing up of codes of conduct pursuant to Article 40(1) and provide an opinion and approve such codes of conduct which provide sufficient safeguards, pursuant to Article 40(5);
texts.text: (m) encourage the drawing up of codes of conduct pursuant to Article 40(1) and provide an opinion and approve such codes of conduct which provide sufficient safeguards, pursuant to Article 40(5);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (n) encourage the establishment of data protection certification mechanisms and of data protection seals and marks pursuant to Article 42(1), and approve the criteria of certification pursuant to Article 42(5);
texts.text: (n) encourage the establishment of data protection certification mechanisms and of data protection seals and marks pursuant to Article 42(1), and approve the criteria of certification pursuant to Article 42(5);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (o) where applicable, carry out a periodic review of certifications issued in accordance with Article 42(7);
texts.text: (o) where applicable, carry out a periodic review of certifications issued in accordance with Article 42(7);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (p) draft and publish the criteria for accreditation of a body for monitoring codes of conduct pursuant to Article 41 and of a certification body pursuant to Article 43;
texts.text: (p) draft and publish the criteria for accreditation of a body for monitoring codes of conduct pursuant to Article 41 and of a certification body pursuant to Article 43;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (q) conduct the accreditation of a body for monitoring codes of conduct pursuant to Article 41 and of a certification body pursuant to Article 43;
texts.text: (q) conduct the accreditation of a body for monitoring codes of conduct pursuant to Article 41 and of a certification body pursuant to Article 43;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (r) authorise contractual clauses and provisions referred to in Article 46(3);
texts.text: (r) authorise contractual clauses and provisions referred to in Article 46(3);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (s) approve binding corporate rules pursuant to Article 47;
texts.text: (s) approve binding corporate rules pursuant to Article 47;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (t) contribute to the activities of the Board;
texts.text: (t) contribute to the activities of the Board;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (u) keep internal records of infringements of this Regulation and of measures taken in accordance with Article 58(2); and
texts.text: (u) keep internal records of infringements of this Regulation and of measures taken in accordance with Article 58(2); and

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (v) fulfil any other tasks related to the protection of personal data.
texts.text: (v) fulfil any other tasks related to the protection of personal data.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Each supervisory authority shall facilitate the submission of complaints referred to in point (f) of paragraph 1 by measures such as a complaint submission form which can also be completed electronically, without excluding other means of communication.
texts.text: Each supervisory authority shall facilitate the submission of complaints referred to in point (f) of paragraph 1 by measures such as a complaint submission form which can also be completed electronically, without excluding other means of communication.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The performance of the tasks of each supervisory authority shall be free of charge for the data subject and, where applicable, for the data protection officer.
texts.text: The performance of the tasks of each supervisory authority shall be free of charge for the data subject and, where applicable, for the data protection officer.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where requests are manifestly unfounded or excessive, in particular because of their repetitive character, the supervisory authority may charge a reasonable fee based on administrative costs, or refuse to act on the request. The supervisory authority shall bear the burden of demonstrating the manifestly unfounded or excessive character of the request.
texts.text: Where requests are manifestly unfounded or excessive, in particular because of their repetitive character, the supervisory authority may charge a reasonable fee based on administrative costs, or refuse to act on the request. The supervisory authority shall bear the burden of demonstrating the manifestly unfounded or excessive character of the request.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 58
texts.text: Article 58

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Powers
texts.text: Powers

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Each supervisory authority shall have all of the following investigative powers:
texts.text: Each supervisory authority shall have all of the following investigative powers:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) to order the controller and the processor, and, where applicable, the controller's or the processor's representative to provide any information it requires for the performance of its tasks;
texts.text: (a) to order the controller and the processor, and, where applicable, the controller's or the processor's representative to provide any information it requires for the performance of its tasks;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) to carry out investigations in the form of data protection audits;
texts.text: (b) to carry out investigations in the form of data protection audits;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) to carry out a review on certifications issued pursuant to Article 42(7);
texts.text: (c) to carry out a review on certifications issued pursuant to Article 42(7);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) to notify the controller or the processor of an alleged infringement of this Regulation;
texts.text: (d) to notify the controller or the processor of an alleged infringement of this Regulation;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) to obtain, from the controller and the processor, access to all personal data and to all information necessary for the performance of its tasks;
texts.text: (e) to obtain, from the controller and the processor, access to all personal data and to all information necessary for the performance of its tasks;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f) to obtain access to any premises of the controller and the processor, including to any data processing equipment and means, in accordance with Union or Member State procedural law.
texts.text: (f) to obtain access to any premises of the controller and the processor, including to any data processing equipment and means, in accordance with Union or Member State procedural law.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Each supervisory authority shall have all of the following corrective powers:
texts.text: Each supervisory authority shall have all of the following corrective powers:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) to issue warnings to a controller or processor that intended processing operations are likely to infringe provisions of this Regulation;
texts.text: (a) to issue warnings to a controller or processor that intended processing operations are likely to infringe provisions of this Regulation;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) to issue reprimands to a controller or a processor where processing operations have infringed provisions of this Regulation;
texts.text: (b) to issue reprimands to a controller or a processor where processing operations have infringed provisions of this Regulation;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) to order the controller or the processor to comply with the data subject's requests to exercise his or her rights pursuant to this Regulation;
texts.text: (c) to order the controller or the processor to comply with the data subject's requests to exercise his or her rights pursuant to this Regulation;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) to order the controller or processor to bring processing operations into compliance with the provisions of this Regulation, where appropriate, in a specified manner and within a specified period;
texts.text: (d) to order the controller or processor to bring processing operations into compliance with the provisions of this Regulation, where appropriate, in a specified manner and within a specified period;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) to order the controller to communicate a personal data breach to the data subject;
texts.text: (e) to order the controller to communicate a personal data breach to the data subject;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f) to impose a temporary or definitive limitation including a ban on processing;
texts.text: (f) to impose a temporary or definitive limitation including a ban on processing;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (g) to order the rectification or erasure of personal data or restriction of processing pursuant to Articles 16, 17 and 18 and the notification of such actions to recipients to whom the personal data have been disclosed pursuant to Article 17(2) and Article 19;
texts.text: (g) to order the rectification or erasure of personal data or restriction of processing pursuant to Articles 16, 17 and 18 and the notification of such actions to recipients to whom the personal data have been disclosed pursuant to Article 17(2) and Article 19;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (h) to withdraw a certification or to order the certification body to withdraw a certification issued pursuant to Articles 42 and 43, or to order the certification body not to issue certification if the requirements for the certifi­ cation are not or are no longer met;
texts.text: (h) to withdraw a certification or to order the certification body to withdraw a certification issued pursuant to Articles 42 and 43, or to order the certification body not to issue certification if the requirements for the certifi cation are not or are no longer met;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (i) to impose an administrative fine pursuant to Article 83, in addition to, or instead of measures referred to in this paragraph, depending on the circumstances of each individual case;
texts.text: (i) to impose an administrative fine pursuant to Article 83, in addition to, or instead of measures referred to in this paragraph, depending on the circumstances of each individual case;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (j) to order the suspension of data flows to a recipient in a third country or to an international organisation.
texts.text: (j) to order the suspension of data flows to a recipient in a third country or to an international organisation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Each supervisory authority shall have all of the following authorisation and advisory powers:
texts.text: Each supervisory authority shall have all of the following authorisation and advisory powers:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) to advise the controller in accordance with the prior consultation procedure referred to in Article 36;
texts.text: (a) to advise the controller in accordance with the prior consultation procedure referred to in Article 36;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) to issue, on its own initiative or on request, opinions to the national parliament, the Member State government or, in accordance with Member State law, to other institutions and bodies as well as to the public on any issue related to the protection of personal data;
texts.text: (b) to issue, on its own initiative or on request, opinions to the national parliament, the Member State government or, in accordance with Member State law, to other institutions and bodies as well as to the public on any issue related to the protection of personal data;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) to authorise processing referred to in Article 36(5), if the law of the Member State requires such prior authorisation;
texts.text: (c) to authorise processing referred to in Article 36(5), if the law of the Member State requires such prior authorisation;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) to issue an opinion and approve draft codes of conduct pursuant to Article 40(5);
texts.text: (d) to issue an opinion and approve draft codes of conduct pursuant to Article 40(5);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) to accredit certification bodies pursuant to Article 43;
texts.text: (e) to accredit certification bodies pursuant to Article 43;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f) to issue certifications and approve criteria of certification in accordance with Article 42(5);
texts.text: (f) to issue certifications and approve criteria of certification in accordance with Article 42(5);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (g) to adopt standard data protection clauses referred to in Article 28(8) and in point (d) of Article 46(2);
texts.text: (g) to adopt standard data protection clauses referred to in Article 28(8) and in point (d) of Article 46(2);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (h) to authorise contractual clauses referred to in point (a) of Article 46(3);
texts.text: (h) to authorise contractual clauses referred to in point (a) of Article 46(3);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (i) to authorise administrative arrangements referred to in point (b) of Article 46(3);
texts.text: (i) to authorise administrative arrangements referred to in point (b) of Article 46(3);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (j) to approve binding corporate rules pursuant to Article 47.
texts.text: (j) to approve binding corporate rules pursuant to Article 47.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The exercise of the powers conferred on the supervisory authority pursuant to this Article shall be subject to appropriate safeguards, including effective judicial remedy and due process, set out in Union and Member State law in accordance with the Charter.
texts.text: The exercise of the powers conferred on the supervisory authority pursuant to this Article shall be subject to appropriate safeguards, including effective judicial remedy and due process, set out in Union and Member State law in accordance with the Charter.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Each Member State shall provide by law that its supervisory authority shall have the power to bring infringements of this Regulation to the attention of the judicial authorities and where appropriate, to commence or engage otherwise in legal proceedings, in order to enforce the provisions of this Regulation.
texts.text: Each Member State shall provide by law that its supervisory authority shall have the power to bring infringements of this Regulation to the attention of the judicial authorities and where appropriate, to commence or engage otherwise in legal proceedings, in order to enforce the provisions of this Regulation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Each Member State may provide by law that its supervisory authority shall have additional powers to those referred to in paragraphs 1, 2 and 3. The exercise of those powers shall not impair the effective operation of Chapter VII.
texts.text: Each Member State may provide by law that its supervisory authority shall have additional powers to those referred to in paragraphs 1, 2 and 3. The exercise of those powers shall not impair the effective operation of Chapter VII.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 59
texts.text: Article 59

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Activity reports
texts.text: Activity reports

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Each  supervisory  authority  shall  draw  up  an  annual  report  on  its  activities,  which  may  include  a  list  of  types  of infringement  notified  and  types  of  measures  taken  in  accordance  with  Article  58(2).  Those  reports  shall  be  transmitted to the national parliament, the government and other authorities as designated by Member State law. They shall be made available to the public, to the Commission and to the Board.
texts.text: Each  supervisory  authority  shall  draw  up  an  annual  report  on  its  activities,  which  may  include  a  list  of  types  of infringement  notified  and  types  of  measures  taken  in  accordance  with  Article  58(2).  Those  reports  shall  be  transmitted to the national parliament, the government and other authorities as designated by Member State law. They shall be made available to the public, to the Commission and to the Board.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: CHAPTER VII
texts.text: CHAPTER VII

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Cooperation and consistency
texts.text: Cooperation and consistency

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Section 1
texts.text: Section 1

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Cooperation
texts.text: Cooperation

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 60
texts.text: Article 60

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Cooperation between  the lead supervisory authority and the other supervisory authorities concerned
texts.text: Cooperation between  the lead supervisory authority and the other supervisory authorities concerned

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The lead supervisory authority shall cooperate with the other supervisory authorities concerned in accordance with this Article in an endeavour to reach consensus. The lead supervisory authority and the supervisory authorities concerned shall exchange all relevant information with each other.
texts.text: The lead supervisory authority shall cooperate with the other supervisory authorities concerned in accordance with this Article in an endeavour to reach consensus. The lead supervisory authority and the supervisory authorities concerned shall exchange all relevant information with each other.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The lead supervisory authority may request at any time other supervisory authorities concerned to provide mutual assistance pursuant to Article 61 and may conduct joint operations pursuant to Article 62, in particular for carrying out investigations or for monitoring the implementation of a measure concerning a controller or processor established in another Member State.
texts.text: The lead supervisory authority may request at any time other supervisory authorities concerned to provide mutual assistance pursuant to Article 61 and may conduct joint operations pursuant to Article 62, in particular for carrying out investigations or for monitoring the implementation of a measure concerning a controller or processor established in another Member State.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The lead supervisory authority shall, without delay, communicate the relevant information on the matter to the other supervisory authorities concerned. It shall without delay submit a draft decision to the other supervisory authorities concerned for their opinion and take due account of their views.
texts.text: The lead supervisory authority shall, without delay, communicate the relevant information on the matter to the other supervisory authorities concerned. It shall without delay submit a draft decision to the other supervisory authorities concerned for their opinion and take due account of their views.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where any of the other supervisory authorities concerned within a period of four weeks after having been consulted in accordance with paragraph 3 of this Article, expresses a relevant and reasoned objection to the draft decision, the lead supervisory authority shall, if it does not follow the relevant and reasoned objection or is of the opinion that the objection is not relevant or reasoned, submit the matter to the consistency mechanism referred to in Article 63.
texts.text: Where any of the other supervisory authorities concerned within a period of four weeks after having been consulted in accordance with paragraph 3 of this Article, expresses a relevant and reasoned objection to the draft decision, the lead supervisory authority shall, if it does not follow the relevant and reasoned objection or is of the opinion that the objection is not relevant or reasoned, submit the matter to the consistency mechanism referred to in Article 63.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where the lead supervisory authority intends to follow the relevant and reasoned objection made, it shall submit to the other supervisory authorities concerned a revised draft decision for their opinion. That revised draft decision shall be subject to the procedure referred to in paragraph 4 within a period of two weeks.
texts.text: Where the lead supervisory authority intends to follow the relevant and reasoned objection made, it shall submit to the other supervisory authorities concerned a revised draft decision for their opinion. That revised draft decision shall be subject to the procedure referred to in paragraph 4 within a period of two weeks.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where none of the other supervisory authorities concerned has objected to the draft decision submitted by the lead supervisory authority within the period referred to in paragraphs 4 and 5, the lead supervisory authority and the supervisory authorities concerned shall be deemed to be in agreement with that draft decision and shall be bound by it.
texts.text: Where none of the other supervisory authorities concerned has objected to the draft decision submitted by the lead supervisory authority within the period referred to in paragraphs 4 and 5, the lead supervisory authority and the supervisory authorities concerned shall be deemed to be in agreement with that draft decision and shall be bound by it.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The lead supervisory authority shall adopt and notify the decision to the main establishment or single establishment of the controller or processor, as the case may be and inform the other supervisory authorities concerned and the Board of the decision in question, including a summary of the relevant facts and grounds. The supervisory authority with which a complaint has been lodged shall inform the complainant on the decision.
texts.text: The lead supervisory authority shall adopt and notify the decision to the main establishment or single establishment of the controller or processor, as the case may be and inform the other supervisory authorities concerned and the Board of the decision in question, including a summary of the relevant facts and grounds. The supervisory authority with which a complaint has been lodged shall inform the complainant on the decision.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: By derogation from paragraph 7, where a complaint is dismissed or rejected, the supervisory authority with which the complaint was lodged shall adopt the decision and notify it to the complainant and shall inform the controller thereof.
texts.text: By derogation from paragraph 7, where a complaint is dismissed or rejected, the supervisory authority with which the complaint was lodged shall adopt the decision and notify it to the complainant and shall inform the controller thereof.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where the lead supervisory authority and the supervisory authorities concerned agree to dismiss or reject parts of a complaint and to act on other parts of that complaint, a separate decision shall be adopted for each of those parts of the matter. The lead supervisory authority shall adopt the decision for the part concerning actions in relation to the controller, shall notify it to the main establishment or single establishment of the controller or processor on the territory of its Member State and shall inform the complainant thereof, while the supervisory authority of the complainant shall adopt the decision for the part concerning dismissal or rejection of that complaint, and shall notify it to that complainant and shall inform the controller or processor thereof.
texts.text: Where the lead supervisory authority and the supervisory authorities concerned agree to dismiss or reject parts of a complaint and to act on other parts of that complaint, a separate decision shall be adopted for each of those parts of the matter. The lead supervisory authority shall adopt the decision for the part concerning actions in relation to the controller, shall notify it to the main establishment or single establishment of the controller or processor on the territory of its Member State and shall inform the complainant thereof, while the supervisory authority of the complainant shall adopt the decision for the part concerning dismissal or rejection of that complaint, and shall notify it to that complainant and shall inform the controller or processor thereof.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: After being notified of the decision of the lead supervisory authority pursuant to paragraphs 7 and 9, the controller or processor shall take the necessary measures to ensure compliance with the decision as regards processing activities in the context of all its establishments in the Union. The controller or processor shall notify the measures taken for complying with the decision to the lead supervisory authority, which shall inform the other supervisory authorities concerned.
texts.text: After being notified of the decision of the lead supervisory authority pursuant to paragraphs 7 and 9, the controller or processor shall take the necessary measures to ensure compliance with the decision as regards processing activities in the context of all its establishments in the Union. The controller or processor shall notify the measures taken for complying with the decision to the lead supervisory authority, which shall inform the other supervisory authorities concerned.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where, in exceptional circumstances, a supervisory authority concerned has reasons to consider that there is an urgent need to act in order to protect the interests of data subjects, the urgency procedure referred to in Article 66 shall apply.
texts.text: Where, in exceptional circumstances, a supervisory authority concerned has reasons to consider that there is an urgent need to act in order to protect the interests of data subjects, the urgency procedure referred to in Article 66 shall apply.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The lead supervisory authority and the other supervisory authorities concerned shall supply the information required under this Article to each other by electronic means, using a standardised format.
texts.text: The lead supervisory authority and the other supervisory authorities concerned shall supply the information required under this Article to each other by electronic means, using a standardised format.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 61
texts.text: Article 61

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Mutual assistance
texts.text: Mutual assistance

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Supervisory authorities shall provide each other with relevant information and mutual assistance in order to implement and apply this Regulation in a consistent manner, and shall put in place measures for effective cooperation with one another. Mutual assistance shall cover, in particular, information requests and supervisory measures, such as requests to carry out prior authorisations and consultations, inspections and investigations.
texts.text: Supervisory authorities shall provide each other with relevant information and mutual assistance in order to implement and apply this Regulation in a consistent manner, and shall put in place measures for effective cooperation with one another. Mutual assistance shall cover, in particular, information requests and supervisory measures, such as requests to carry out prior authorisations and consultations, inspections and investigations.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Each supervisory authority shall take all appropriate measures required to reply to a request of another supervisory authority without undue delay and no later than one month after receiving the request. Such measures may include, in particular, the transmission of relevant information on the conduct of an investigation.
texts.text: Each supervisory authority shall take all appropriate measures required to reply to a request of another supervisory authority without undue delay and no later than one month after receiving the request. Such measures may include, in particular, the transmission of relevant information on the conduct of an investigation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Requests for assistance shall contain all the necessary information, including the purpose of and reasons for the request. Information exchanged shall be used only for the purpose for which it was requested.
texts.text: Requests for assistance shall contain all the necessary information, including the purpose of and reasons for the request. Information exchanged shall be used only for the purpose for which it was requested.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The requested supervisory authority shall not refuse to comply with the request unless:
texts.text: The requested supervisory authority shall not refuse to comply with the request unless:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) it is not competent for the subject-matter of the request or for the measures it is requested to execute; or
texts.text: (a) it is not competent for the subject-matter of the request or for the measures it is requested to execute; or

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) compliance with the request would infringe this Regulation or Union or Member State law to which the supervisory authority receiving the request is subject.
texts.text: (b) compliance with the request would infringe this Regulation or Union or Member State law to which the supervisory authority receiving the request is subject.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The requested supervisory authority shall inform the requesting supervisory authority of the results or, as the case may be, of the progress of the measures taken in order to respond to the request. The requested supervisory authority shall provide reasons for any refusal to comply with a request pursuant to paragraph 4.
texts.text: The requested supervisory authority shall inform the requesting supervisory authority of the results or, as the case may be, of the progress of the measures taken in order to respond to the request. The requested supervisory authority shall provide reasons for any refusal to comply with a request pursuant to paragraph 4.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Requested supervisory authorities shall, as a rule, supply the information requested by other supervisory authorities by electronic means, using a standardised format.
texts.text: Requested supervisory authorities shall, as a rule, supply the information requested by other supervisory authorities by electronic means, using a standardised format.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Requested supervisory authorities shall not charge a fee for any action taken by them pursuant to a request for mutual assistance. Supervisory authorities may agree on rules to indemnify each other for specific expenditure arising from the provision of mutual assistance in exceptional circumstances.
texts.text: Requested supervisory authorities shall not charge a fee for any action taken by them pursuant to a request for mutual assistance. Supervisory authorities may agree on rules to indemnify each other for specific expenditure arising from the provision of mutual assistance in exceptional circumstances.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where a supervisory authority does not provide the information referred to in paragraph 5 of this Article within one month of receiving the request of another supervisory authority, the requesting supervisory authority may adopt a provisional measure on the territory of its Member State in accordance with Article 55(1). In that case, the urgent need to act under Article 66(1) shall be presumed to be met and require an urgent binding decision from the Board pursuant to Article 66(2).
texts.text: Where a supervisory authority does not provide the information referred to in paragraph 5 of this Article within one month of receiving the request of another supervisory authority, the requesting supervisory authority may adopt a provisional measure on the territory of its Member State in accordance with Article 55(1). In that case, the urgent need to act under Article 66(1) shall be presumed to be met and require an urgent binding decision from the Board pursuant to Article 66(2).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Commission may, by means of implementing acts, specify the format and procedures for mutual assistance referred to in this Article and the arrangements for the exchange of information by electronic means between supervisory authorities, and between supervisory authorities and the Board, in particular the standardised format referred to in paragraph 6 of this Article. Those implementing acts shall be adopted in accordance with the examination procedure referred to in Article 93(2).
texts.text: The Commission may, by means of implementing acts, specify the format and procedures for mutual assistance referred to in this Article and the arrangements for the exchange of information by electronic means between supervisory authorities, and between supervisory authorities and the Board, in particular the standardised format referred to in paragraph 6 of this Article. Those implementing acts shall be adopted in accordance with the examination procedure referred to in Article 93(2).

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 62
texts.text: Article 62

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Joint operations of supervisory authorities
texts.text: Joint operations of supervisory authorities

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The supervisory authorities shall, where appropriate, conduct joint operations including joint investigations and joint enforcement measures in which members or staff of the supervisory authorities of other Member States are involved.
texts.text: The supervisory authorities shall, where appropriate, conduct joint operations including joint investigations and joint enforcement measures in which members or staff of the supervisory authorities of other Member States are involved.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where the controller or processor has establishments in several Member States or where a significant number of data subjects in more than one Member State are likely to be substantially affected by processing operations, a supervisory authority of each of those Member States shall have the right to participate in joint operations. The supervisory authority which is competent pursuant to Article 56(1) or (4) shall invite the supervisory authority of each of those Member States to take part in the joint operations and shall respond without delay to the request of a supervisory authority to participate.
texts.text: Where the controller or processor has establishments in several Member States or where a significant number of data subjects in more than one Member State are likely to be substantially affected by processing operations, a supervisory authority of each of those Member States shall have the right to participate in joint operations. The supervisory authority which is competent pursuant to Article 56(1) or (4) shall invite the supervisory authority of each of those Member States to take part in the joint operations and shall respond without delay to the request of a supervisory authority to participate.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A supervisory authority may, in accordance with Member State law, and with the seconding supervisory authority's authorisation, confer powers, including investigative powers on the seconding supervisory authority's members or staff involved in joint operations or, in so far as the law of the Member State of the host supervisory authority permits, allow the seconding supervisory authority's members or staff to exercise their investigative powers in accordance with the law of the Member State of the seconding supervisory authority. Such investigative powers may be exercised only under the guidance and in the presence of members or staff of the host supervisory authority. The seconding supervisory authority's members or staff shall be subject to the Member State law of the host supervisory authority.
texts.text: A supervisory authority may, in accordance with Member State law, and with the seconding supervisory authority's authorisation, confer powers, including investigative powers on the seconding supervisory authority's members or staff involved in joint operations or, in so far as the law of the Member State of the host supervisory authority permits, allow the seconding supervisory authority's members or staff to exercise their investigative powers in accordance with the law of the Member State of the seconding supervisory authority. Such investigative powers may be exercised only under the guidance and in the presence of members or staff of the host supervisory authority. The seconding supervisory authority's members or staff shall be subject to the Member State law of the host supervisory authority.

texts.label: list_item
