texts.orig: Where, in accordance with paragraph 1, staff of a seconding supervisory authority operate in another Member State, the Member State of the host supervisory authority shall assume responsibility for their actions, including liability, for any damage caused by them during their operations, in accordance with the law of the Member State in whose territory they are operating.
texts.text: Where, in accordance with paragraph 1, staff of a seconding supervisory authority operate in another Member State, the Member State of the host supervisory authority shall assume responsibility for their actions, including liability, for any damage caused by them during their operations, in accordance with the law of the Member State in whose territory they are operating.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Member State in whose territory the damage was caused shall make good such damage under the conditions applicable to damage caused by its own staff. The Member State of the seconding supervisory authority whose staff has caused damage to any person in the territory of another Member State shall reimburse that other Member State in full any sums it has paid to the persons entitled on their behalf.
texts.text: The Member State in whose territory the damage was caused shall make good such damage under the conditions applicable to damage caused by its own staff. The Member State of the seconding supervisory authority whose staff has caused damage to any person in the territory of another Member State shall reimburse that other Member State in full any sums it has paid to the persons entitled on their behalf.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Without prejudice to the exercise of its rights vis-à-vis third parties and with the exception of paragraph 5, each Member State shall refrain, in the case provided for in paragraph 1, from requesting reimbursement from another Member State in relation to damage referred to in paragraph 4.
texts.text: Without prejudice to the exercise of its rights vis-à-vis third parties and with the exception of paragraph 5, each Member State shall refrain, in the case provided for in paragraph 1, from requesting reimbursement from another Member State in relation to damage referred to in paragraph 4.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where a joint operation is intended and a supervisory authority does not, within one month, comply with the obligation laid down in the second sentence of paragraph 2 of this Article, the other supervisory authorities may adopt a provisional measure on the territory of its Member State in accordance with Article 55. In that case, the urgent need to act under Article 66(1) shall be presumed to be met and require an opinion or an urgent binding decision from the Board pursuant to Article 66(2).
texts.text: Where a joint operation is intended and a supervisory authority does not, within one month, comply with the obligation laid down in the second sentence of paragraph 2 of this Article, the other supervisory authorities may adopt a provisional measure on the territory of its Member State in accordance with Article 55. In that case, the urgent need to act under Article 66(1) shall be presumed to be met and require an opinion or an urgent binding decision from the Board pursuant to Article 66(2).

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Section 2
texts.text: Section 2

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Consistency
texts.text: Consistency

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 63
texts.text: Article 63

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Consistency mechanism
texts.text: Consistency mechanism

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In  order  to contribute to the  consistent  application of  this Regulation throughout the Union, the supervisory authorities shall  cooperate  with  each  other  and,  where  relevant,  with  the  Commission,  through  the  consistency  mechanism  as  set out in this Section.
texts.text: In  order  to contribute to the  consistent  application of  this Regulation throughout the Union, the supervisory authorities shall  cooperate  with  each  other  and,  where  relevant,  with  the  Commission,  through  the  consistency  mechanism  as  set out in this Section.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 64
texts.text: Article 64

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Opinion of the Board
texts.text: Opinion of the Board

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Board shall issue an opinion where a competent supervisory authority intends to adopt any of the measures below. To that end, the competent supervisory authority shall communicate the draft decision to the Board, when it:
texts.text: The Board shall issue an opinion where a competent supervisory authority intends to adopt any of the measures below. To that end, the competent supervisory authority shall communicate the draft decision to the Board, when it:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) aims to adopt a list of the processing operations subject to the requirement for a data protection impact assessment pursuant to Article 35(4);
texts.text: (a) aims to adopt a list of the processing operations subject to the requirement for a data protection impact assessment pursuant to Article 35(4);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) concerns a matter pursuant to Article 40(7) whether a draft code of conduct or an amendment or extension to a code of conduct complies with this Regulation;
texts.text: (b) concerns a matter pursuant to Article 40(7) whether a draft code of conduct or an amendment or extension to a code of conduct complies with this Regulation;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) aims to approve the criteria for accreditation of a body pursuant to Article 41(3) or a certification body pursuant to Article 43(3);
texts.text: (c) aims to approve the criteria for accreditation of a body pursuant to Article 41(3) or a certification body pursuant to Article 43(3);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) aims to determine standard data protection clauses referred to in point (d) of Article 46(2) and in Article 28(8);
texts.text: (d) aims to determine standard data protection clauses referred to in point (d) of Article 46(2) and in Article 28(8);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) aims to authorise contractual clauses referred to in point (a) of Article 46(3); or
texts.text: (e) aims to authorise contractual clauses referred to in point (a) of Article 46(3); or

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f) aims to approve binding corporate rules within the meaning of Article 47.
texts.text: (f) aims to approve binding corporate rules within the meaning of Article 47.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Any supervisory authority, the Chair of the Board or the Commission may request that any matter of general application or producing effects in more than one Member State be examined by the Board with a view to obtaining an opinion, in particular where a competent supervisory authority does not comply with the obligations for mutual assistance in accordance with Article 61 or for joint operations in accordance with Article 62.
texts.text: Any supervisory authority, the Chair of the Board or the Commission may request that any matter of general application or producing effects in more than one Member State be examined by the Board with a view to obtaining an opinion, in particular where a competent supervisory authority does not comply with the obligations for mutual assistance in accordance with Article 61 or for joint operations in accordance with Article 62.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In the cases referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2, the Board shall issue an opinion on the matter submitted to it provided that it has not already issued an opinion on the same matter. That opinion shall be adopted within eight weeks by simple majority of the members of the Board. That period may be extended by a further six weeks, taking into account the complexity of the subject matter. Regarding the draft decision referred to in paragraph 1 circulated to the members of the Board in accordance with paragraph 5, a member which has not objected within a reasonable period indicated by the Chair, shall be deemed to be in agreement with the draft decision.
texts.text: In the cases referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2, the Board shall issue an opinion on the matter submitted to it provided that it has not already issued an opinion on the same matter. That opinion shall be adopted within eight weeks by simple majority of the members of the Board. That period may be extended by a further six weeks, taking into account the complexity of the subject matter. Regarding the draft decision referred to in paragraph 1 circulated to the members of the Board in accordance with paragraph 5, a member which has not objected within a reasonable period indicated by the Chair, shall be deemed to be in agreement with the draft decision.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Supervisory authorities and the Commission shall, without undue delay, communicate by electronic means to the Board, using a standardised format any relevant information, including as the case may be a summary of the facts, the draft decision, the grounds which make the enactment of such measure necessary, and the views of other supervisory authorities concerned.
texts.text: Supervisory authorities and the Commission shall, without undue delay, communicate by electronic means to the Board, using a standardised format any relevant information, including as the case may be a summary of the facts, the draft decision, the grounds which make the enactment of such measure necessary, and the views of other supervisory authorities concerned.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Chair of the Board shall, without undue, delay inform by electronic means:
texts.text: The Chair of the Board shall, without undue, delay inform by electronic means:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) the members of the Board and the Commission of any relevant information which has been communicated to it using a standardised format. The secretariat of the Board shall, where necessary, provide translations of relevant information; and
texts.text: (a) the members of the Board and the Commission of any relevant information which has been communicated to it using a standardised format. The secretariat of the Board shall, where necessary, provide translations of relevant information; and

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) the supervisory authority referred to, as the case may be, in paragraphs 1 and 2, and the Commission of the opinion and make it public.
texts.text: (b) the supervisory authority referred to, as the case may be, in paragraphs 1 and 2, and the Commission of the opinion and make it public.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The competent supervisory authority shall not adopt its draft decision referred to in paragraph 1 within the period referred to in paragraph 3.
texts.text: The competent supervisory authority shall not adopt its draft decision referred to in paragraph 1 within the period referred to in paragraph 3.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The supervisory authority referred to in paragraph 1 shall take utmost account of the opinion of the Board and shall, within two weeks after receiving the opinion, communicate to the Chair of the Board by electronic means whether it will maintain or amend its draft decision and, if any, the amended draft decision, using a standardised format.
texts.text: The supervisory authority referred to in paragraph 1 shall take utmost account of the opinion of the Board and shall, within two weeks after receiving the opinion, communicate to the Chair of the Board by electronic means whether it will maintain or amend its draft decision and, if any, the amended draft decision, using a standardised format.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where the supervisory authority concerned informs the Chair of the Board within the period referred to in paragraph 7 of this Article that it does not intend to follow the opinion of the Board, in whole or in part, providing the relevant grounds, Article 65(1) shall apply.
texts.text: Where the supervisory authority concerned informs the Chair of the Board within the period referred to in paragraph 7 of this Article that it does not intend to follow the opinion of the Board, in whole or in part, providing the relevant grounds, Article 65(1) shall apply.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 65
texts.text: Article 65

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Dispute resolution by the Board
texts.text: Dispute resolution by the Board

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In order to ensure the correct and consistent application of this Regulation in individual cases, the Board shall adopt a binding decision in the following cases:
texts.text: In order to ensure the correct and consistent application of this Regulation in individual cases, the Board shall adopt a binding decision in the following cases:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) where, in a case referred to in Article 60(4), a supervisory authority concerned has raised a relevant and reasoned objection to a draft decision of the lead authority or the lead authority has rejected such an objection as being not relevant or reasoned. The binding decision shall concern all the matters which are the subject of the relevant and reasoned objection, in particular whether there is an infringement of this Regulation;
texts.text: (a) where, in a case referred to in Article 60(4), a supervisory authority concerned has raised a relevant and reasoned objection to a draft decision of the lead authority or the lead authority has rejected such an objection as being not relevant or reasoned. The binding decision shall concern all the matters which are the subject of the relevant and reasoned objection, in particular whether there is an infringement of this Regulation;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) where there are conflicting views on which of the supervisory authorities concerned is competent for the main establishment;
texts.text: (b) where there are conflicting views on which of the supervisory authorities concerned is competent for the main establishment;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) where a competent supervisory authority does not request the opinion of the Board in the cases referred to in Article 64(1), or does not follow the opinion of the Board issued under Article 64. In that case, any supervisory authority concerned or the Commission may communicate the matter to the Board.
texts.text: (c) where a competent supervisory authority does not request the opinion of the Board in the cases referred to in Article 64(1), or does not follow the opinion of the Board issued under Article 64. In that case, any supervisory authority concerned or the Commission may communicate the matter to the Board.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The decision referred to in paragraph 1 shall be adopted within one month from the referral of the subject-matter by a two-thirds majority of the members of the Board. That period may be extended by a further month on account of the complexity of the subject-matter. The decision referred to in paragraph 1 shall be reasoned and addressed to the lead supervisory authority and all the supervisory authorities concerned and binding on them.
texts.text: The decision referred to in paragraph 1 shall be adopted within one month from the referral of the subject-matter by a two-thirds majority of the members of the Board. That period may be extended by a further month on account of the complexity of the subject-matter. The decision referred to in paragraph 1 shall be reasoned and addressed to the lead supervisory authority and all the supervisory authorities concerned and binding on them.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where the Board has been unable to adopt a decision within the periods referred to in paragraph 2, it shall adopt its decision within two weeks following the expiration of the second month referred to in paragraph 2 by a simple majority of the members of the Board. Where the members of the Board are split, the decision shall by adopted by the vote of its Chair.
texts.text: Where the Board has been unable to adopt a decision within the periods referred to in paragraph 2, it shall adopt its decision within two weeks following the expiration of the second month referred to in paragraph 2 by a simple majority of the members of the Board. Where the members of the Board are split, the decision shall by adopted by the vote of its Chair.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The supervisory authorities concerned shall not adopt a decision on the subject matter submitted to the Board under paragraph 1 during the periods referred to in paragraphs 2 and 3.
texts.text: The supervisory authorities concerned shall not adopt a decision on the subject matter submitted to the Board under paragraph 1 during the periods referred to in paragraphs 2 and 3.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Chair of the Board shall notify, without undue delay, the decision referred to in paragraph 1 to the supervisory authorities concerned. It shall inform the Commission thereof. The decision shall be published on the website of the Board without delay after the supervisory authority has notified the final decision referred to in paragraph 6.
texts.text: The Chair of the Board shall notify, without undue delay, the decision referred to in paragraph 1 to the supervisory authorities concerned. It shall inform the Commission thereof. The decision shall be published on the website of the Board without delay after the supervisory authority has notified the final decision referred to in paragraph 6.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The lead supervisory authority or, as the case may be, the supervisory authority with which the complaint has been lodged shall adopt its final decision on the basis of the decision referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article, without undue delay and at the latest by one month after the Board has notified its decision. The lead supervisory authority or, as the case may be, the supervisory authority with which the complaint has been lodged, shall inform the Board of the date when its final decision is notified respectively to the controller or the processor and to the data subject. The final decision of the supervisory authorities concerned shall be adopted under the terms of Article 60(7), (8) and (9). The final decision shall refer to the decision referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article and shall specify that the decision referred to in that paragraph will be published on the website of the Board in accordance with paragraph 5 of this Article. The final decision shall attach the decision referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article.
texts.text: The lead supervisory authority or, as the case may be, the supervisory authority with which the complaint has been lodged shall adopt its final decision on the basis of the decision referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article, without undue delay and at the latest by one month after the Board has notified its decision. The lead supervisory authority or, as the case may be, the supervisory authority with which the complaint has been lodged, shall inform the Board of the date when its final decision is notified respectively to the controller or the processor and to the data subject. The final decision of the supervisory authorities concerned shall be adopted under the terms of Article 60(7), (8) and (9). The final decision shall refer to the decision referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article and shall specify that the decision referred to in that paragraph will be published on the website of the Board in accordance with paragraph 5 of this Article. The final decision shall attach the decision referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 66
texts.text: Article 66

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Urgency procedure
texts.text: Urgency procedure

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In exceptional circumstances, where a supervisory authority concerned considers that there is an urgent need to act in order to protect the rights and freedoms of data subjects, it may, by way of derogation from the consistency mechanism referred to in Articles 63, 64 and 65 or the procedure referred to in Article 60, immediately adopt provisional measures intended to produce legal effects on its own territory with a specified period of validity which shall not exceed three months. The supervisory authority shall, without delay, communicate those measures and the reasons for adopting them to the other supervisory authorities concerned, to the Board and to the Commission.
texts.text: In exceptional circumstances, where a supervisory authority concerned considers that there is an urgent need to act in order to protect the rights and freedoms of data subjects, it may, by way of derogation from the consistency mechanism referred to in Articles 63, 64 and 65 or the procedure referred to in Article 60, immediately adopt provisional measures intended to produce legal effects on its own territory with a specified period of validity which shall not exceed three months. The supervisory authority shall, without delay, communicate those measures and the reasons for adopting them to the other supervisory authorities concerned, to the Board and to the Commission.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where a supervisory authority has taken a measure pursuant to paragraph 1 and considers that final measures need urgently be adopted, it may request an urgent opinion or an urgent binding decision from the Board, giving reasons for requesting such opinion or decision.
texts.text: Where a supervisory authority has taken a measure pursuant to paragraph 1 and considers that final measures need urgently be adopted, it may request an urgent opinion or an urgent binding decision from the Board, giving reasons for requesting such opinion or decision.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Any supervisory authority may request an urgent opinion or an urgent binding decision, as the case may be, from the Board where a competent supervisory authority has not taken an appropriate measure in a situation where there is an urgent need to act, in order to protect the rights and freedoms of data subjects, giving reasons for requesting such opinion or decision, including for the urgent need to act.
texts.text: Any supervisory authority may request an urgent opinion or an urgent binding decision, as the case may be, from the Board where a competent supervisory authority has not taken an appropriate measure in a situation where there is an urgent need to act, in order to protect the rights and freedoms of data subjects, giving reasons for requesting such opinion or decision, including for the urgent need to act.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: By derogation from Article 64(3) and Article 65(2), an urgent opinion or an urgent binding decision referred to in paragraphs 2 and 3 of this Article shall be adopted within two weeks by simple majority of the members of the Board.
texts.text: By derogation from Article 64(3) and Article 65(2), an urgent opinion or an urgent binding decision referred to in paragraphs 2 and 3 of this Article shall be adopted within two weeks by simple majority of the members of the Board.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 67
texts.text: Article 67

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Exchange of information
texts.text: Exchange of information

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  Commission  may  adopt  implementing  acts  of  general  scope  in  order  to  specify  the  arrangements  for  the  exchange of  information by electronic means between supervisory authorities, and between supervisory authorities and the Board, in particular  the standardised format referred to in Article 64.
texts.text: The  Commission  may  adopt  implementing  acts  of  general  scope  in  order  to  specify  the  arrangements  for  the  exchange of  information by electronic means between supervisory authorities, and between supervisory authorities and the Board, in particular  the standardised format referred to in Article 64.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Those implementing acts shall be adopted in accordance with the examination procedure referred to in Article 93(2).
texts.text: Those implementing acts shall be adopted in accordance with the examination procedure referred to in Article 93(2).

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Section 3
texts.text: Section 3

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: European data protection board
texts.text: European data protection board

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 68
texts.text: Article 68

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: European Data Protection Board
texts.text: European Data Protection Board

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The European Data Protection Board (the 'Board') is hereby established as a body of the Union and shall have legal personality.
texts.text: The European Data Protection Board (the 'Board') is hereby established as a body of the Union and shall have legal personality.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Board shall be represented by its Chair.
texts.text: The Board shall be represented by its Chair.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Board shall be composed of the head of one supervisory authority of each Member State and of the European Data Protection Supervisor, or their respective representatives.
texts.text: The Board shall be composed of the head of one supervisory authority of each Member State and of the European Data Protection Supervisor, or their respective representatives.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where in a Member State more than one supervisory authority is responsible for monitoring the application of the provisions pursuant to this Regulation, a joint representative shall be appointed in accordance with that Member State's law.
texts.text: Where in a Member State more than one supervisory authority is responsible for monitoring the application of the provisions pursuant to this Regulation, a joint representative shall be appointed in accordance with that Member State's law.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Commission shall have the right to participate in the activities and meetings of the Board without voting right. The Commission shall designate a representative. The Chair of the Board shall communicate to the Commission the activities of the Board.
texts.text: The Commission shall have the right to participate in the activities and meetings of the Board without voting right. The Commission shall designate a representative. The Chair of the Board shall communicate to the Commission the activities of the Board.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In the cases referred to in Article 65, the European Data Protection Supervisor shall have voting rights only on decisions which concern principles and rules applicable to the Union institutions, bodies, offices and agencies which correspond in substance to those of this Regulation.
texts.text: In the cases referred to in Article 65, the European Data Protection Supervisor shall have voting rights only on decisions which concern principles and rules applicable to the Union institutions, bodies, offices and agencies which correspond in substance to those of this Regulation.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 69
texts.text: Article 69

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Independence
texts.text: Independence

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Board shall act independently when performing its tasks or exercising its powers pursuant to Articles 70 and 71.
texts.text: The Board shall act independently when performing its tasks or exercising its powers pursuant to Articles 70 and 71.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Without prejudice to requests by the Commission referred to in point (b) of Article 70(1) and in Article 70(2), the Board shall, in the performance of its tasks or the exercise of its powers, neither seek nor take instructions from anybody.
texts.text: Without prejudice to requests by the Commission referred to in point (b) of Article 70(1) and in Article 70(2), the Board shall, in the performance of its tasks or the exercise of its powers, neither seek nor take instructions from anybody.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 70
texts.text: Article 70

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Tasks of the Board
texts.text: Tasks of the Board

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Board shall ensure the consistent application of this Regulation. To that end, the Board shall, on its own initiative or, where relevant, at the request of the Commission, in particular:
texts.text: The Board shall ensure the consistent application of this Regulation. To that end, the Board shall, on its own initiative or, where relevant, at the request of the Commission, in particular:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) monitor and ensure the correct application of this Regulation in the cases provided for in Articles 64 and 65 without prejudice to the tasks of national supervisory authorities;
texts.text: (a) monitor and ensure the correct application of this Regulation in the cases provided for in Articles 64 and 65 without prejudice to the tasks of national supervisory authorities;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) advise the Commission on any issue related to the protection of personal data in the Union, including on any proposed amendment of this Regulation;
texts.text: (b) advise the Commission on any issue related to the protection of personal data in the Union, including on any proposed amendment of this Regulation;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) advise the Commission on the format and procedures for the exchange of information between controllers, processors and supervisory authorities for binding corporate rules;
texts.text: (c) advise the Commission on the format and procedures for the exchange of information between controllers, processors and supervisory authorities for binding corporate rules;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) issue guidelines, recommendations, and best practices on procedures for erasing links, copies or replications of personal data from publicly available communication services as referred to in Article 17(2);
texts.text: (d) issue guidelines, recommendations, and best practices on procedures for erasing links, copies or replications of personal data from publicly available communication services as referred to in Article 17(2);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) examine, on its own initiative, on request of one of its members or on request of the Commission, any question covering the application of this Regulation and issue guidelines, recommendations and best practices in order to encourage consistent application of this Regulation;
texts.text: (e) examine, on its own initiative, on request of one of its members or on request of the Commission, any question covering the application of this Regulation and issue guidelines, recommendations and best practices in order to encourage consistent application of this Regulation;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f) issue guidelines, recommendations and best practices in accordance with point (e) of this paragraph for further specifying the criteria and conditions for decisions based on profiling pursuant to Article 22(2);
texts.text: (f) issue guidelines, recommendations and best practices in accordance with point (e) of this paragraph for further specifying the criteria and conditions for decisions based on profiling pursuant to Article 22(2);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (g) issue guidelines, recommendations and best practices in accordance with point (e) of this paragraph for establishing the personal data breaches and determining the undue delay referred to in Article 33(1) and (2) and for the particular circumstances in which a controller or a processor is required to notify the personal data breach;
texts.text: (g) issue guidelines, recommendations and best practices in accordance with point (e) of this paragraph for establishing the personal data breaches and determining the undue delay referred to in Article 33(1) and (2) and for the particular circumstances in which a controller or a processor is required to notify the personal data breach;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (h) issue guidelines, recommendations and best practices in accordance with point (e) of this paragraph as to the circumstances in which a personal data breach is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of the natural persons referred to in Article 34(1).
texts.text: (h) issue guidelines, recommendations and best practices in accordance with point (e) of this paragraph as to the circumstances in which a personal data breach is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of the natural persons referred to in Article 34(1).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (i) issue guidelines, recommendations and best practices in accordance with point (e) of this paragraph for the purpose of further specifying the criteria and requirements for personal data transfers based on binding corporate rules adhered to by controllers and binding corporate rules adhered to by processors and on further necessary requirements to ensure the protection of personal data of the data subjects concerned referred to in Article 47;
texts.text: (i) issue guidelines, recommendations and best practices in accordance with point (e) of this paragraph for the purpose of further specifying the criteria and requirements for personal data transfers based on binding corporate rules adhered to by controllers and binding corporate rules adhered to by processors and on further necessary requirements to ensure the protection of personal data of the data subjects concerned referred to in Article 47;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (j) issue guidelines, recommendations and best practices in accordance with point (e) of this paragraph for the purpose of further specifying the criteria and requirements for the personal data transfers on the basis of Article 49(1);
texts.text: (j) issue guidelines, recommendations and best practices in accordance with point (e) of this paragraph for the purpose of further specifying the criteria and requirements for the personal data transfers on the basis of Article 49(1);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (k) draw up guidelines for supervisory authorities concerning the application of measures referred to in Article 58(1), (2) and (3) and the setting of administrative fines pursuant to Article 83;
texts.text: (k) draw up guidelines for supervisory authorities concerning the application of measures referred to in Article 58(1), (2) and (3) and the setting of administrative fines pursuant to Article 83;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (l) review the practical application of the guidelines, recommendations and best practices referred to in points (e) and (f);
texts.text: (l) review the practical application of the guidelines, recommendations and best practices referred to in points (e) and (f);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (m) issue guidelines, recommendations and best practices in accordance with point (e) of this paragraph for establishing common procedures for reporting by natural persons of infringements of this Regulation pursuant to Article 54(2);
texts.text: (m) issue guidelines, recommendations and best practices in accordance with point (e) of this paragraph for establishing common procedures for reporting by natural persons of infringements of this Regulation pursuant to Article 54(2);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (n) encourage the drawing-up of codes of conduct and the establishment of data protection certification mechanisms and data protection seals and marks pursuant to Articles 40 and 42;
texts.text: (n) encourage the drawing-up of codes of conduct and the establishment of data protection certification mechanisms and data protection seals and marks pursuant to Articles 40 and 42;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (o) carry out the accreditation of certification bodies and its periodic review pursuant to Article 43 and maintain a public register of accredited bodies pursuant to Article 43(6) and of the accredited controllers or processors established in third countries pursuant to Article 42(7);
texts.text: (o) carry out the accreditation of certification bodies and its periodic review pursuant to Article 43 and maintain a public register of accredited bodies pursuant to Article 43(6) and of the accredited controllers or processors established in third countries pursuant to Article 42(7);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (p) specify the requirements referred to in Article 43(3) with a view to the accreditation of certification bodies under Article 42;
texts.text: (p) specify the requirements referred to in Article 43(3) with a view to the accreditation of certification bodies under Article 42;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (q) provide the Commission with an opinion on the certification requirements referred to in Article 43(8);
texts.text: (q) provide the Commission with an opinion on the certification requirements referred to in Article 43(8);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (r) provide the Commission with an opinion on the icons referred to in Article 12(7);
texts.text: (r) provide the Commission with an opinion on the icons referred to in Article 12(7);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (s) provide the Commission with an opinion for the assessment of the adequacy of the level of protection in a third country or international organisation, including for the assessment whether a third country, a territory or one or more specified sectors within that third country, or an international organisation no longer ensures an adequate level of protection. To that end, the Commission shall provide the Board with all necessary documentation, including correspondence with the government of the third country, with regard to that third country, territory or specified sector, or with the international organisation.
texts.text: (s) provide the Commission with an opinion for the assessment of the adequacy of the level of protection in a third country or international organisation, including for the assessment whether a third country, a territory or one or more specified sectors within that third country, or an international organisation no longer ensures an adequate level of protection. To that end, the Commission shall provide the Board with all necessary documentation, including correspondence with the government of the third country, with regard to that third country, territory or specified sector, or with the international organisation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (t) issue opinions on draft decisions of supervisory authorities pursuant to the consistency mechanism referred to in Article 64(1), on matters submitted pursuant to Article 64(2) and to issue binding decisions pursuant to Article 65, including in cases referred to in Article 66;
texts.text: (t) issue opinions on draft decisions of supervisory authorities pursuant to the consistency mechanism referred to in Article 64(1), on matters submitted pursuant to Article 64(2) and to issue binding decisions pursuant to Article 65, including in cases referred to in Article 66;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (u) promote the cooperation and the effective bilateral and multilateral exchange of information and best practices between the supervisory authorities;
texts.text: (u) promote the cooperation and the effective bilateral and multilateral exchange of information and best practices between the supervisory authorities;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (v) promote common training programmes and facilitate personnel exchanges between the supervisory authorities and, where appropriate, with the supervisory authorities of third countries or with international organisations;
texts.text: (v) promote common training programmes and facilitate personnel exchanges between the supervisory authorities and, where appropriate, with the supervisory authorities of third countries or with international organisations;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (w) promote the exchange of knowledge and documentation on data protection legislation and practice with data protection supervisory authorities worldwide.
texts.text: (w) promote the exchange of knowledge and documentation on data protection legislation and practice with data protection supervisory authorities worldwide.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (x) issue opinions on codes of conduct drawn up at Union level pursuant to Article 40(9); and
texts.text: (x) issue opinions on codes of conduct drawn up at Union level pursuant to Article 40(9); and

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (y) maintain a publicly accessible electronic register of decisions taken by supervisory authorities and courts on issues handled in the consistency mechanism.
texts.text: (y) maintain a publicly accessible electronic register of decisions taken by supervisory authorities and courts on issues handled in the consistency mechanism.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where the Commission requests advice from the Board, it may indicate a time limit, taking into account the urgency of the matter.
texts.text: Where the Commission requests advice from the Board, it may indicate a time limit, taking into account the urgency of the matter.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Board shall forward its opinions, guidelines, recommendations, and best practices to the Commission and to the committee referred to in Article 93 and make them public.
texts.text: The Board shall forward its opinions, guidelines, recommendations, and best practices to the Commission and to the committee referred to in Article 93 and make them public.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Board shall, where appropriate, consult interested parties and give them the opportunity to comment within a reasonable period. The Board shall, without prejudice to Article 76, make the results of the consultation procedure publicly available.
texts.text: The Board shall, where appropriate, consult interested parties and give them the opportunity to comment within a reasonable period. The Board shall, without prejudice to Article 76, make the results of the consultation procedure publicly available.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 71
texts.text: Article 71

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Reports
texts.text: Reports

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Board shall draw up an annual report regarding the protection of natural persons with regard to processing in the Union and, where relevant, in third countries and international organisations. The report shall be made public and be transmitted to the European Parliament, to the Council and to the Commission.
texts.text: The Board shall draw up an annual report regarding the protection of natural persons with regard to processing in the Union and, where relevant, in third countries and international organisations. The report shall be made public and be transmitted to the European Parliament, to the Council and to the Commission.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The annual report shall include a review of the practical application of the guidelines, recommendations and best practices referred to in point (l) of Article 70(1) as well as of the binding decisions referred to in Article 65.
texts.text: The annual report shall include a review of the practical application of the guidelines, recommendations and best practices referred to in point (l) of Article 70(1) as well as of the binding decisions referred to in Article 65.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 72
texts.text: Article 72

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Procedure
texts.text: Procedure

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Board shall take decisions by a simple majority of its members, unless otherwise provided for in this Regulation.
texts.text: The Board shall take decisions by a simple majority of its members, unless otherwise provided for in this Regulation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Board shall adopt its own rules of procedure by a two-thirds majority of its members and organise its own operational arrangements.
texts.text: The Board shall adopt its own rules of procedure by a two-thirds majority of its members and organise its own operational arrangements.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 73
texts.text: Article 73

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Chair
texts.text: Chair

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Board shall elect a chair and two deputy chairs from amongst its members by simple majority.
texts.text: The Board shall elect a chair and two deputy chairs from amongst its members by simple majority.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The term of office of the Chair and of the deputy chairs shall be five years and be renewable once.
texts.text: The term of office of the Chair and of the deputy chairs shall be five years and be renewable once.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 74
texts.text: Article 74

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Tasks of the Chair
texts.text: Tasks of the Chair

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Chair shall have the following tasks:
texts.text: The Chair shall have the following tasks:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) to convene the meetings of the Board and prepare its agenda;
texts.text: (a) to convene the meetings of the Board and prepare its agenda;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) to notify decisions adopted by the Board pursuant to Article 65 to the lead supervisory authority and the supervisory authorities concerned;
texts.text: (b) to notify decisions adopted by the Board pursuant to Article 65 to the lead supervisory authority and the supervisory authorities concerned;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) to ensure the timely performance of the tasks of the Board, in particular in relation to the consistency mechanism referred to in Article 63.
texts.text: (c) to ensure the timely performance of the tasks of the Board, in particular in relation to the consistency mechanism referred to in Article 63.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Board shall lay down the allocation of tasks between the Chair and the deputy chairs in its rules of procedure.
texts.text: The Board shall lay down the allocation of tasks between the Chair and the deputy chairs in its rules of procedure.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 75
texts.text: Article 75

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Secretariat
texts.text: Secretariat

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Board shall have a secretariat, which shall be provided by the European Data Protection Supervisor.
texts.text: The Board shall have a secretariat, which shall be provided by the European Data Protection Supervisor.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The secretariat shall perform its tasks exclusively under the instructions of the Chair of the Board.
texts.text: The secretariat shall perform its tasks exclusively under the instructions of the Chair of the Board.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The staff of the European Data Protection Supervisor involved in carrying out the tasks conferred on the Board by this Regulation shall be subject to separate reporting lines from the staff involved in carrying out tasks conferred on the European Data Protection Supervisor.
texts.text: The staff of the European Data Protection Supervisor involved in carrying out the tasks conferred on the Board by this Regulation shall be subject to separate reporting lines from the staff involved in carrying out tasks conferred on the European Data Protection Supervisor.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where appropriate, the Board and the European Data Protection Supervisor shall establish and publish a Memorandum of Understanding implementing this Article, determining the terms of their cooperation, and applicable to the staff of the European Data Protection Supervisor involved in carrying out the tasks conferred on the Board by this Regulation.
texts.text: Where appropriate, the Board and the European Data Protection Supervisor shall establish and publish a Memorandum of Understanding implementing this Article, determining the terms of their cooperation, and applicable to the staff of the European Data Protection Supervisor involved in carrying out the tasks conferred on the Board by this Regulation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The secretariat shall provide analytical, administrative and logistical support to the Board.
texts.text: The secretariat shall provide analytical, administrative and logistical support to the Board.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The secretariat shall be responsible in particular for:
texts.text: The secretariat shall be responsible in particular for:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) the day-to-day business of the Board;
texts.text: (a) the day-to-day business of the Board;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) communication between the members of the Board, its Chair and the Commission;
texts.text: (b) communication between the members of the Board, its Chair and the Commission;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) communication with other institutions and the public;
texts.text: (c) communication with other institutions and the public;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) the use of electronic means for the internal and external communication;
texts.text: (d) the use of electronic means for the internal and external communication;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) the translation of relevant information;
texts.text: (e) the translation of relevant information;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f) the preparation and follow-up of the meetings of the Board;
texts.text: (f) the preparation and follow-up of the meetings of the Board;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (g) the preparation, drafting and publication of opinions, decisions on the settlement of disputes between supervisory authorities and other texts adopted by the Board.
texts.text: (g) the preparation, drafting and publication of opinions, decisions on the settlement of disputes between supervisory authorities and other texts adopted by the Board.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 76
texts.text: Article 76

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Confidentiality
texts.text: Confidentiality

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The discussions of the Board shall be confidential where the Board deems it necessary, as provided for in its rules of procedure.
texts.text: The discussions of the Board shall be confidential where the Board deems it necessary, as provided for in its rules of procedure.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Access to documents submitted to members of the Board, experts and representatives of third parties shall be governed by Regulation (EC) No 1049/2001 of the European Parliament and of the Council ( 1 ).
texts.text: Access to documents submitted to members of the Board, experts and representatives of third parties shall be governed by Regulation (EC) No 1049/2001 of the European Parliament and of the Council ( 1 ).

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: CHAPTER VIII
texts.text: CHAPTER VIII

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Remedies, liability and penalties
texts.text: Remedies, liability and penalties

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 77
texts.text: Article 77

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority
texts.text: Right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Without prejudice to any other administrative or judicial remedy, every data subject shall have the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority, in particular in the Member State of his or her habitual residence, place of work or place of the alleged infringement if the data subject considers that the processing of personal data relating to him or her infringes this Regulation.
texts.text: Without prejudice to any other administrative or judicial remedy, every data subject shall have the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority, in particular in the Member State of his or her habitual residence, place of work or place of the alleged infringement if the data subject considers that the processing of personal data relating to him or her infringes this Regulation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The supervisory authority with which the complaint has been lodged shall inform the complainant on the progress and the outcome of the complaint including the possibility of a judicial remedy pursuant to Article 78.
texts.text: The supervisory authority with which the complaint has been lodged shall inform the complainant on the progress and the outcome of the complaint including the possibility of a judicial remedy pursuant to Article 78.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 78
texts.text: Article 78

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Right to an effective judicial remedy against a supervisory authority
texts.text: Right to an effective judicial remedy against a supervisory authority

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Without prejudice to any other administrative or non-judicial remedy, each natural or legal person shall have the right to an effective judicial remedy against a legally binding decision of a supervisory authority concerning them.
texts.text: Without prejudice to any other administrative or non-judicial remedy, each natural or legal person shall have the right to an effective judicial remedy against a legally binding decision of a supervisory authority concerning them.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Without prejudice to any other administrative or non-judicial remedy, each data subject shall have the right to a an effective judicial remedy where the supervisory authority which is competent pursuant to Articles 55 and 56 does not handle a complaint or does not inform the data subject within three months on the progress or outcome of the complaint lodged pursuant to Article 77.
texts.text: Without prejudice to any other administrative or non-judicial remedy, each data subject shall have the right to a an effective judicial remedy where the supervisory authority which is competent pursuant to Articles 55 and 56 does not handle a complaint or does not inform the data subject within three months on the progress or outcome of the complaint lodged pursuant to Article 77.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Proceedings against a supervisory authority shall be brought before the courts of the Member State where the supervisory authority is established.
texts.text: Proceedings against a supervisory authority shall be brought before the courts of the Member State where the supervisory authority is established.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where proceedings are brought against a decision of a supervisory authority which was preceded by an opinion or a decision of the Board in the consistency mechanism, the supervisory authority shall forward that opinion or decision to the court.
texts.text: Where proceedings are brought against a decision of a supervisory authority which was preceded by an opinion or a decision of the Board in the consistency mechanism, the supervisory authority shall forward that opinion or decision to the court.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 79
texts.text: Article 79

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Right to an effective judicial remedy against a controller or processor
texts.text: Right to an effective judicial remedy against a controller or processor

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Without prejudice to any available administrative or non-judicial remedy, including the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority pursuant to Article 77, each data subject shall have the right to an effective judicial remedy where he or she considers that his or her rights under this Regulation have been infringed as a result of the processing of his or her personal data in non-compliance with this Regulation.
texts.text: Without prejudice to any available administrative or non-judicial remedy, including the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority pursuant to Article 77, each data subject shall have the right to an effective judicial remedy where he or she considers that his or her rights under this Regulation have been infringed as a result of the processing of his or her personal data in non-compliance with this Regulation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Proceedings against a controller or a processor shall be brought before the courts of the Member State where the controller or processor has an establishment. Alternatively, such proceedings may be brought before the courts of the Member State where the data subject has his or her habitual residence, unless the controller or processor is a public authority of a Member State acting in the exercise of its public powers.
texts.text: Proceedings against a controller or a processor shall be brought before the courts of the Member State where the controller or processor has an establishment. Alternatively, such proceedings may be brought before the courts of the Member State where the data subject has his or her habitual residence, unless the controller or processor is a public authority of a Member State acting in the exercise of its public powers.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: ( 1 ) Regulation (EC) No 1049/2001 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 30 May 2001 regarding public access to European Parliament, Council and Commission documents (OJ L 145, 31.5.2001, p. 43).
texts.text: ( 1 ) Regulation (EC) No 1049/2001 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 30 May 2001 regarding public access to European Parliament, Council and Commission documents (OJ L 145, 31.5.2001, p. 43).

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 80
texts.text: Article 80

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Representation of data subjects
texts.text: Representation of data subjects

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The data subject shall have the right to mandate a not-for-profit body, organisation or association which has been properly constituted in accordance with the law of a Member State, has statutory objectives which are in the public interest, and is active in the field of the protection of data subjects' rights and freedoms with regard to the protection of their personal data to lodge the complaint on his or her behalf, to exercise the rights referred to in Articles 77, 78 and 79 on his or her behalf, and to exercise the right to receive compensation referred to in Article 82 on his or her behalf where provided for by Member State law.
texts.text: The data subject shall have the right to mandate a not-for-profit body, organisation or association which has been properly constituted in accordance with the law of a Member State, has statutory objectives which are in the public interest, and is active in the field of the protection of data subjects' rights and freedoms with regard to the protection of their personal data to lodge the complaint on his or her behalf, to exercise the rights referred to in Articles 77, 78 and 79 on his or her behalf, and to exercise the right to receive compensation referred to in Article 82 on his or her behalf where provided for by Member State law.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Member States may provide that any body, organisation or association referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article, in­ dependently of a data subject's mandate, has the right to lodge, in that Member State, a complaint with the supervisory authority which is competent pursuant to Article 77 and to exercise the rights referred to in Articles 78 and 79 if it considers that the rights of a data subject under this Regulation have been infringed as a result of the processing.
texts.text: Member States may provide that any body, organisation or association referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article, in dependently of a data subject's mandate, has the right to lodge, in that Member State, a complaint with the supervisory authority which is competent pursuant to Article 77 and to exercise the rights referred to in Articles 78 and 79 if it considers that the rights of a data subject under this Regulation have been infringed as a result of the processing.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 81
texts.text: Article 81

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Suspension of proceedings
texts.text: Suspension of proceedings

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where a competent court of a Member State has information on proceedings, concerning the same subject matter as regards processing by the same controller or processor, that are pending in a court in another Member State, it shall contact that court in the other Member State to confirm the existence of such proceedings.
texts.text: Where a competent court of a Member State has information on proceedings, concerning the same subject matter as regards processing by the same controller or processor, that are pending in a court in another Member State, it shall contact that court in the other Member State to confirm the existence of such proceedings.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where proceedings concerning the same subject matter as regards processing of the same controller or processor are pending in a court in another Member State, any competent court other than the court first seized may suspend its proceedings.
texts.text: Where proceedings concerning the same subject matter as regards processing of the same controller or processor are pending in a court in another Member State, any competent court other than the court first seized may suspend its proceedings.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where those proceedings are pending at first instance, any court other than the court first seized may also, on the application of one of the parties, decline jurisdiction if the court first seized has jurisdiction over the actions in question and its law permits the consolidation thereof.
texts.text: Where those proceedings are pending at first instance, any court other than the court first seized may also, on the application of one of the parties, decline jurisdiction if the court first seized has jurisdiction over the actions in question and its law permits the consolidation thereof.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 82
texts.text: Article 82

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Right to compensation and liability
texts.text: Right to compensation and liability

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Any person who has suffered material or non-material damage as a result of an infringement of this Regulation shall have the right to receive compensation from the controller or processor for the damage suffered.
texts.text: Any person who has suffered material or non-material damage as a result of an infringement of this Regulation shall have the right to receive compensation from the controller or processor for the damage suffered.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Any controller involved in processing shall be liable for the damage caused by processing which infringes this Regulation. A processor shall be liable for the damage caused by processing only where it has not complied with obligations of this Regulation specifically directed to processors or where it has acted outside or contrary to lawful instructions of the controller.
texts.text: Any controller involved in processing shall be liable for the damage caused by processing which infringes this Regulation. A processor shall be liable for the damage caused by processing only where it has not complied with obligations of this Regulation specifically directed to processors or where it has acted outside or contrary to lawful instructions of the controller.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A controller or processor shall be exempt from liability under paragraph 2 if it proves that it is not in any way responsible for the event giving rise to the damage.
texts.text: A controller or processor shall be exempt from liability under paragraph 2 if it proves that it is not in any way responsible for the event giving rise to the damage.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where more than one controller or processor, or both a controller and a processor, are involved in the same processing and where they are, under paragraphs 2 and 3, responsible for any damage caused by processing, each controller or processor shall be held liable for the entire damage in order to ensure effective compensation of the data subject.
texts.text: Where more than one controller or processor, or both a controller and a processor, are involved in the same processing and where they are, under paragraphs 2 and 3, responsible for any damage caused by processing, each controller or processor shall be held liable for the entire damage in order to ensure effective compensation of the data subject.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where a controller or processor has, in accordance with paragraph 4, paid full compensation for the damage suffered, that controller or processor shall be entitled to claim back from the other controllers or processors involved in the same processing that part of the compensation corresponding to their part of responsibility for the damage, in accordance with the conditions set out in paragraph 2.
texts.text: Where a controller or processor has, in accordance with paragraph 4, paid full compensation for the damage suffered, that controller or processor shall be entitled to claim back from the other controllers or processors involved in the same processing that part of the compensation corresponding to their part of responsibility for the damage, in accordance with the conditions set out in paragraph 2.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Court proceedings for exercising the right to receive compensation shall be brought before the courts competent under the law of the Member State referred to in Article 79(2).
texts.text: Court proceedings for exercising the right to receive compensation shall be brought before the courts competent under the law of the Member State referred to in Article 79(2).

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 83
texts.text: Article 83

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: General conditions for imposing administrative fines
texts.text: General conditions for imposing administrative fines

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Each supervisory authority shall ensure that the imposition of administrative fines pursuant to this Article in respect of infringements of this Regulation referred to in paragraphs 4, 5 and 6 shall in each individual case be effective, proportionate and dissuasive.
texts.text: Each supervisory authority shall ensure that the imposition of administrative fines pursuant to this Article in respect of infringements of this Regulation referred to in paragraphs 4, 5 and 6 shall in each individual case be effective, proportionate and dissuasive.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Administrative fines shall, depending on the circumstances of each individual case, be imposed in addition to, or instead of, measures referred to in points (a) to (h) and (j) of Article 58(2). When deciding whether to impose an administrative fine and deciding on the amount of the administrative fine in each individual case due regard shall be given to the following:
texts.text: Administrative fines shall, depending on the circumstances of each individual case, be imposed in addition to, or instead of, measures referred to in points (a) to (h) and (j) of Article 58(2). When deciding whether to impose an administrative fine and deciding on the amount of the administrative fine in each individual case due regard shall be given to the following:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) the nature, gravity and duration of the infringement taking into account the nature scope or purpose of the processing concerned as well as the number of data subjects affected and the level of damage suffered by them;
texts.text: (a) the nature, gravity and duration of the infringement taking into account the nature scope or purpose of the processing concerned as well as the number of data subjects affected and the level of damage suffered by them;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) the intentional or negligent character of the infringement;
texts.text: (b) the intentional or negligent character of the infringement;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) any action taken by the controller or processor to mitigate the damage suffered by data subjects;
texts.text: (c) any action taken by the controller or processor to mitigate the damage suffered by data subjects;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) the degree of responsibility of the controller or processor taking into account technical and organisational measures implemented by them pursuant to Articles 25 and 32;
texts.text: (d) the degree of responsibility of the controller or processor taking into account technical and organisational measures implemented by them pursuant to Articles 25 and 32;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) any relevant previous infringements by the controller or processor;
texts.text: (e) any relevant previous infringements by the controller or processor;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f) the degree of cooperation with the supervisory authority, in order to remedy the infringement and mitigate the possible adverse effects of the infringement;
texts.text: (f) the degree of cooperation with the supervisory authority, in order to remedy the infringement and mitigate the possible adverse effects of the infringement;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (g) the categories of personal data affected by the infringement;
texts.text: (g) the categories of personal data affected by the infringement;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (h) the manner in which the infringement became known to the supervisory authority, in particular whether, and if so to what extent, the controller or processor notified the infringement;
texts.text: (h) the manner in which the infringement became known to the supervisory authority, in particular whether, and if so to what extent, the controller or processor notified the infringement;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (i) where measures referred to in Article 58(2) have previously been ordered against the controller or processor concerned with regard to the same subject-matter, compliance with those measures;
texts.text: (i) where measures referred to in Article 58(2) have previously been ordered against the controller or processor concerned with regard to the same subject-matter, compliance with those measures;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (j) adherence to approved codes of conduct pursuant to Article 40 or approved certification mechanisms pursuant to Article 42; and
texts.text: (j) adherence to approved codes of conduct pursuant to Article 40 or approved certification mechanisms pursuant to Article 42; and

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (k) any other aggravating or mitigating factor applicable to the circumstances of the case, such as financial benefits gained, or losses avoided, directly or indirectly, from the infringement.
texts.text: (k) any other aggravating or mitigating factor applicable to the circumstances of the case, such as financial benefits gained, or losses avoided, directly or indirectly, from the infringement.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: If a controller or processor intentionally or negligently, for the same or linked processing operations, infringes several provisions of this Regulation, the total amount of the administrative fine shall not exceed the amount specified for the gravest infringement.
texts.text: If a controller or processor intentionally or negligently, for the same or linked processing operations, infringes several provisions of this Regulation, the total amount of the administrative fine shall not exceed the amount specified for the gravest infringement.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Infringements of the following provisions shall, in accordance with paragraph 2, be subject to administrative fines up to 10 000 000 EUR, or in the case of an undertaking, up to 2 % of the total worldwide annual turnover of the preceding financial year, whichever is higher:
texts.text: Infringements of the following provisions shall, in accordance with paragraph 2, be subject to administrative fines up to 10 000 000 EUR, or in the case of an undertaking, up to 2 % of the total worldwide annual turnover of the preceding financial year, whichever is higher:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) the obligations of the controller and the processor pursuant to Articles 8, 11, 25 to 39 and 42 and 43;
texts.text: (a) the obligations of the controller and the processor pursuant to Articles 8, 11, 25 to 39 and 42 and 43;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) the obligations of the certification body pursuant to Articles 42 and 43;
texts.text: (b) the obligations of the certification body pursuant to Articles 42 and 43;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) the obligations of the monitoring body pursuant to Article 41(4).
texts.text: (c) the obligations of the monitoring body pursuant to Article 41(4).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Infringements of the following provisions shall, in accordance with paragraph 2, be subject to administrative fines up to 20 000 000 EUR, or in the case of an undertaking, up to 4 % of the total worldwide annual turnover of the preceding financial year, whichever is higher:
texts.text: Infringements of the following provisions shall, in accordance with paragraph 2, be subject to administrative fines up to 20 000 000 EUR, or in the case of an undertaking, up to 4 % of the total worldwide annual turnover of the preceding financial year, whichever is higher:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) the basic principles for processing, including conditions for consent, pursuant to Articles 5, 6, 7 and 9;
texts.text: (a) the basic principles for processing, including conditions for consent, pursuant to Articles 5, 6, 7 and 9;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) the data subjects' rights pursuant to Articles 12 to 22;
texts.text: (b) the data subjects' rights pursuant to Articles 12 to 22;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) the transfers of personal data to a recipient in a third country or an international organisation pursuant to Articles 44 to 49;
texts.text: (c) the transfers of personal data to a recipient in a third country or an international organisation pursuant to Articles 44 to 49;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) any obligations pursuant to Member State law adopted under Chapter IX;
texts.text: (d) any obligations pursuant to Member State law adopted under Chapter IX;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) non-compliance with an order or a temporary or definitive limitation on processing or the suspension of data flows by the supervisory authority pursuant to Article 58(2) or failure to provide access in violation of Article 58(1).
texts.text: (e) non-compliance with an order or a temporary or definitive limitation on processing or the suspension of data flows by the supervisory authority pursuant to Article 58(2) or failure to provide access in violation of Article 58(1).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Non-compliance with an order by the supervisory authority as referred to in Article 58(2) shall, in accordance with paragraph 2 of this Article, be subject to administrative fines up to 20 000 000 EUR, or in the case of an undertaking, up to 4 % of the total worldwide annual turnover of the preceding financial year, whichever is higher.
texts.text: Non-compliance with an order by the supervisory authority as referred to in Article 58(2) shall, in accordance with paragraph 2 of this Article, be subject to administrative fines up to 20 000 000 EUR, or in the case of an undertaking, up to 4 % of the total worldwide annual turnover of the preceding financial year, whichever is higher.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Without prejudice to the corrective powers of supervisory authorities pursuant to Article 58(2), each Member State may lay down the rules on whether and to what extent administrative fines may be imposed on public authorities and bodies established in that Member State.
texts.text: Without prejudice to the corrective powers of supervisory authorities pursuant to Article 58(2), each Member State may lay down the rules on whether and to what extent administrative fines may be imposed on public authorities and bodies established in that Member State.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The exercise by the supervisory authority of its powers under this Article shall be subject to appropriate procedural safeguards in accordance with Union and Member State law, including effective judicial remedy and due process.
texts.text: The exercise by the supervisory authority of its powers under this Article shall be subject to appropriate procedural safeguards in accordance with Union and Member State law, including effective judicial remedy and due process.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where the legal system of the Member State does not provide for administrative fines, this Article may be applied in such a manner that the fine is initiated by the competent supervisory authority and imposed by competent national courts, while ensuring that those legal remedies are effective and have an equivalent effect to the administrative fines imposed by supervisory authorities. In any event, the fines imposed shall be effective, proportionate and dissuasive. Those Member States shall notify to the Commission the provisions of their laws which they adopt pursuant to this paragraph by 25 May 2018 and, without delay, any subsequent amendment law or amendment affecting them.
texts.text: Where the legal system of the Member State does not provide for administrative fines, this Article may be applied in such a manner that the fine is initiated by the competent supervisory authority and imposed by competent national courts, while ensuring that those legal remedies are effective and have an equivalent effect to the administrative fines imposed by supervisory authorities. In any event, the fines imposed shall be effective, proportionate and dissuasive. Those Member States shall notify to the Commission the provisions of their laws which they adopt pursuant to this paragraph by 25 May 2018 and, without delay, any subsequent amendment law or amendment affecting them.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 84
texts.text: Article 84

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Penalties
texts.text: Penalties

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Member States shall lay down the rules on other penalties applicable to infringements of this Regulation in particular for infringements which are not subject to administrative fines pursuant to Article 83, and shall take all measures necessary to ensure that they are implemented. Such penalties shall be effective, proportionate and dissuasive.
texts.text: Member States shall lay down the rules on other penalties applicable to infringements of this Regulation in particular for infringements which are not subject to administrative fines pursuant to Article 83, and shall take all measures necessary to ensure that they are implemented. Such penalties shall be effective, proportionate and dissuasive.

texts.label: list_item
