texts.orig: Each Member State shall notify to the Commission the provisions of its law which it adopts pursuant to paragraph 1, by 25 May 2018 and, without delay, any subsequent amendment affecting them.
texts.text: Each Member State shall notify to the Commission the provisions of its law which it adopts pursuant to paragraph 1, by 25 May 2018 and, without delay, any subsequent amendment affecting them.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: CHAPTER IX
texts.text: CHAPTER IX

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Provisions relating to specific processing situations
texts.text: Provisions relating to specific processing situations

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 85
texts.text: Article 85

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Processing and freedom of expression and information
texts.text: Processing and freedom of expression and information

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Member States shall by law reconcile the right to the protection of personal data pursuant to this Regulation with the right to freedom of expression and information, including processing for journalistic purposes and the purposes of academic, artistic or literary expression.
texts.text: Member States shall by law reconcile the right to the protection of personal data pursuant to this Regulation with the right to freedom of expression and information, including processing for journalistic purposes and the purposes of academic, artistic or literary expression.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: For processing carried out for journalistic purposes or the purpose of academic artistic or literary expression, Member States shall provide for exemptions or derogations from Chapter II (principles), Chapter III (rights of the data subject), Chapter IV (controller and processor), Chapter V (transfer of personal data to third countries or international organisations), Chapter VI (independent supervisory authorities), Chapter VII (cooperation and consistency) and Chapter IX (specific data processing situations) if they are necessary to reconcile the right to the protection of personal data with the freedom of expression and information.
texts.text: For processing carried out for journalistic purposes or the purpose of academic artistic or literary expression, Member States shall provide for exemptions or derogations from Chapter II (principles), Chapter III (rights of the data subject), Chapter IV (controller and processor), Chapter V (transfer of personal data to third countries or international organisations), Chapter VI (independent supervisory authorities), Chapter VII (cooperation and consistency) and Chapter IX (specific data processing situations) if they are necessary to reconcile the right to the protection of personal data with the freedom of expression and information.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Each Member State shall notify to the Commission the provisions of its law which it has adopted pursuant to paragraph 2 and, without delay, any subsequent amendment law or amendment affecting them.
texts.text: Each Member State shall notify to the Commission the provisions of its law which it has adopted pursuant to paragraph 2 and, without delay, any subsequent amendment law or amendment affecting them.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 86
texts.text: Article 86

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Processing and public access to official documents
texts.text: Processing and public access to official documents

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Personal data in official  documents held by a public authority or a public body or a private body for  the performance of a  task  carried  out  in  the  public  interest  may  be  disclosed  by the  authority or  body in  accordance with Union or Member State  law  to which the  public  authority or  body  is  subject  in  order  to  reconcile  public  access  to  official  documents  with the right to the protection of personal data pursuant to this Regulation.
texts.text: Personal data in official  documents held by a public authority or a public body or a private body for  the performance of a  task  carried  out  in  the  public  interest  may  be  disclosed  by the  authority or  body in  accordance with Union or Member State  law  to which the  public  authority or  body  is  subject  in  order  to  reconcile  public  access  to  official  documents  with the right to the protection of personal data pursuant to this Regulation.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 87
texts.text: Article 87

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Processing of the national identification number
texts.text: Processing of the national identification number

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Member  States  may  further  determine  the  specific  conditions  for  the  processing  of  a  national  identification  number  or any  other  identifier  of  general  application.  In  that  case  the  national  identification  number  or  any  other  identifier  of general  application  shall  be  used  only  under  appropriate  safeguards  for  the  rights  and  freedoms  of  the  data  subject pursuant to this Regulation.
texts.text: Member  States  may  further  determine  the  specific  conditions  for  the  processing  of  a  national  identification  number  or any  other  identifier  of  general  application.  In  that  case  the  national  identification  number  or  any  other  identifier  of general  application  shall  be  used  only  under  appropriate  safeguards  for  the  rights  and  freedoms  of  the  data  subject pursuant to this Regulation.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 88
texts.text: Article 88

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Processing in the context of employment
texts.text: Processing in the context of employment

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Member States may, by law or by collective agreements, provide for more specific rules to ensure the protection of the rights and freedoms in respect of the processing of employees' personal data in the employment context, in particular for the purposes of the recruitment, the performance of the contract of employment, including discharge of obligations laid down by law or by collective agreements, management, planning and organisation of work, equality and diversity in the workplace, health and safety at work, protection of employer's or customer's property and for the purposes of the exercise and enjoyment, on an individual or collective basis, of rights and benefits related to employment, and for the purpose of the termination of the employment relationship.
texts.text: Member States may, by law or by collective agreements, provide for more specific rules to ensure the protection of the rights and freedoms in respect of the processing of employees' personal data in the employment context, in particular for the purposes of the recruitment, the performance of the contract of employment, including discharge of obligations laid down by law or by collective agreements, management, planning and organisation of work, equality and diversity in the workplace, health and safety at work, protection of employer's or customer's property and for the purposes of the exercise and enjoyment, on an individual or collective basis, of rights and benefits related to employment, and for the purpose of the termination of the employment relationship.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Those rules shall include suitable and specific measures to safeguard the data subject's human dignity, legitimate interests and fundamental rights, with particular regard to the transparency of processing, the transfer of personal data within a group of undertakings, or a group of enterprises engaged in a joint economic activity and monitoring systems at the work place.
texts.text: Those rules shall include suitable and specific measures to safeguard the data subject's human dignity, legitimate interests and fundamental rights, with particular regard to the transparency of processing, the transfer of personal data within a group of undertakings, or a group of enterprises engaged in a joint economic activity and monitoring systems at the work place.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Each Member State shall notify to the Commission those provisions of its law which it adopts pursuant to paragraph 1, by 25 May 2018 and, without delay, any subsequent amendment affecting them.
texts.text: Each Member State shall notify to the Commission those provisions of its law which it adopts pursuant to paragraph 1, by 25 May 2018 and, without delay, any subsequent amendment affecting them.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 89
texts.text: Article 89

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Safeguards  and  derogations  relating  to  processing  for  archiving  purposes  in  the  public  interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes
texts.text: Safeguards  and  derogations  relating  to  processing  for  archiving  purposes  in  the  public  interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Processing for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes, shall be subject to appropriate safeguards, in accordance with this Regulation, for the rights and freedoms of the data subject. Those safeguards shall ensure that technical and organisational measures are in place in particular in
texts.text: Processing for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes, shall be subject to appropriate safeguards, in accordance with this Regulation, for the rights and freedoms of the data subject. Those safeguards shall ensure that technical and organisational measures are in place in particular in

texts.label: text
texts.orig: order  to  ensure  respect  for  the  principle  of  data  minimisation.  Those  measures  may  include  pseudonymisation  provided that  those  purposes  can  be  fulfilled  in  that  manner.  Where  those  purposes  can  be  fulfilled  by  further  processing  which does not permit or no longer permits the identification of data subjects, those purposes shall be fulfilled in that manner.
texts.text: order  to  ensure  respect  for  the  principle  of  data  minimisation.  Those  measures  may  include  pseudonymisation  provided that  those  purposes  can  be  fulfilled  in  that  manner.  Where  those  purposes  can  be  fulfilled  by  further  processing  which does not permit or no longer permits the identification of data subjects, those purposes shall be fulfilled in that manner.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where personal data are processed for scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes, Union or Member State law may provide for derogations from the rights referred to in Articles 15, 16, 18 and 21 subject to the conditions and safeguards referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article in so far as such rights are likely to render impossible or seriously impair the achievement of the specific purposes, and such derogations are necessary for the fulfilment of those purposes.
texts.text: Where personal data are processed for scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes, Union or Member State law may provide for derogations from the rights referred to in Articles 15, 16, 18 and 21 subject to the conditions and safeguards referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article in so far as such rights are likely to render impossible or seriously impair the achievement of the specific purposes, and such derogations are necessary for the fulfilment of those purposes.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where personal data are processed for archiving purposes in the public interest, Union or Member State law may provide for derogations from the rights referred to in Articles 15, 16, 18, 19, 20 and 21 subject to the conditions and safeguards referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article in so far as such rights are likely to render impossible or seriously impair the achievement of the specific purposes, and such derogations are necessary for the fulfilment of those purposes.
texts.text: Where personal data are processed for archiving purposes in the public interest, Union or Member State law may provide for derogations from the rights referred to in Articles 15, 16, 18, 19, 20 and 21 subject to the conditions and safeguards referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article in so far as such rights are likely to render impossible or seriously impair the achievement of the specific purposes, and such derogations are necessary for the fulfilment of those purposes.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where processing referred to in paragraphs 2 and 3 serves at the same time another purpose, the derogations shall apply only to processing for the purposes referred to in those paragraphs.
texts.text: Where processing referred to in paragraphs 2 and 3 serves at the same time another purpose, the derogations shall apply only to processing for the purposes referred to in those paragraphs.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 90
texts.text: Article 90

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Obligations of secrecy
texts.text: Obligations of secrecy

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Member States may adopt specific rules to set out the powers of the supervisory authorities laid down in points (e) and (f) of Article 58(1) in relation to controllers or processors that are subject, under Union or Member State law or rules established by national competent bodies, to an obligation of professional secrecy or other equivalent obligations of secrecy where this is necessary and proportionate to reconcile the right of the protection of personal data with the obligation of secrecy. Those rules shall apply only with regard to personal data which the controller or processor has received as a result of or has obtained in an activity covered by that obligation of secrecy.
texts.text: Member States may adopt specific rules to set out the powers of the supervisory authorities laid down in points (e) and (f) of Article 58(1) in relation to controllers or processors that are subject, under Union or Member State law or rules established by national competent bodies, to an obligation of professional secrecy or other equivalent obligations of secrecy where this is necessary and proportionate to reconcile the right of the protection of personal data with the obligation of secrecy. Those rules shall apply only with regard to personal data which the controller or processor has received as a result of or has obtained in an activity covered by that obligation of secrecy.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Each Member State shall notify to the Commission the rules adopted pursuant to paragraph 1, by 25 May 2018 and, without delay, any subsequent amendment affecting them.
texts.text: Each Member State shall notify to the Commission the rules adopted pursuant to paragraph 1, by 25 May 2018 and, without delay, any subsequent amendment affecting them.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 91
texts.text: Article 91

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Existing data protection rules of churches and religious associations
texts.text: Existing data protection rules of churches and religious associations

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where in a Member State, churches and religious associations or communities apply, at the time of entry into force of this Regulation, comprehensive rules relating to the protection of natural persons with regard to processing, such rules may continue to apply, provided that they are brought into line with this Regulation.
texts.text: Where in a Member State, churches and religious associations or communities apply, at the time of entry into force of this Regulation, comprehensive rules relating to the protection of natural persons with regard to processing, such rules may continue to apply, provided that they are brought into line with this Regulation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Churches and religious associations which apply comprehensive rules in accordance with paragraph 1 of this Article shall be subject to the supervision of an independent supervisory authority, which may be specific, provided that it fulfils the conditions laid down in Chapter VI of this Regulation.
texts.text: Churches and religious associations which apply comprehensive rules in accordance with paragraph 1 of this Article shall be subject to the supervision of an independent supervisory authority, which may be specific, provided that it fulfils the conditions laid down in Chapter VI of this Regulation.

texts.text: CHAPTER X

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Delegated acts and implementing acts
texts.text: Delegated acts and implementing acts

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 92
texts.text: Article 92

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Exercise of the delegation
texts.text: Exercise of the delegation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The power to adopt delegated acts is conferred on the Commission subject to the conditions laid down in this Article.
texts.text: The power to adopt delegated acts is conferred on the Commission subject to the conditions laid down in this Article.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The delegation of power referred to in Article 12(8) and Article 43(8) shall be conferred on the Commission for an indeterminate period of time from 24 May 2016.
texts.text: The delegation of power referred to in Article 12(8) and Article 43(8) shall be conferred on the Commission for an indeterminate period of time from 24 May 2016.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The delegation of power referred to in Article 12(8) and Article 43(8) may be revoked at any time by the European Parliament or by the Council. A decision of revocation shall put an end to the delegation of power specified in that decision. It shall take effect the day following that of its publication in the Official Journal of the European Union or at a later date specified therein. It shall not affect the validity of any delegated acts already in force.
texts.text: The delegation of power referred to in Article 12(8) and Article 43(8) may be revoked at any time by the European Parliament or by the Council. A decision of revocation shall put an end to the delegation of power specified in that decision. It shall take effect the day following that of its publication in the Official Journal of the European Union or at a later date specified therein. It shall not affect the validity of any delegated acts already in force.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: As soon as it adopts a delegated act, the Commission shall notify it simultaneously to the European Parliament and to the Council.
texts.text: As soon as it adopts a delegated act, the Commission shall notify it simultaneously to the European Parliament and to the Council.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A delegated act adopted pursuant to Article 12(8) and Article 43(8) shall enter into force only if no objection has been expressed by either the European Parliament or the Council within a period of three months of notification of that act to the European Parliament and the Council or if, before the expiry of that period, the European Parliament and the Council have both informed the Commission that they will not object. That period shall be extended by three months at the initiative of the European Parliament or of the Council.
texts.text: A delegated act adopted pursuant to Article 12(8) and Article 43(8) shall enter into force only if no objection has been expressed by either the European Parliament or the Council within a period of three months of notification of that act to the European Parliament and the Council or if, before the expiry of that period, the European Parliament and the Council have both informed the Commission that they will not object. That period shall be extended by three months at the initiative of the European Parliament or of the Council.

texts.orig: Article 93

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Committee procedure
texts.text: Committee procedure

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Commission shall be assisted by a committee. That committee shall be a committee within the meaning of Regulation (EU) No 182/2011.
texts.text: The Commission shall be assisted by a committee. That committee shall be a committee within the meaning of Regulation (EU) No 182/2011.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where reference is made to this paragraph, Article 5 of Regulation (EU) No 182/2011 shall apply.
texts.text: Where reference is made to this paragraph, Article 5 of Regulation (EU) No 182/2011 shall apply.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where reference is made to this paragraph, Article 8 of Regulation (EU) No 182/2011, in conjunction with Article 5 thereof, shall apply.
texts.text: Where reference is made to this paragraph, Article 8 of Regulation (EU) No 182/2011, in conjunction with Article 5 thereof, shall apply.

texts.text: Final provisions

texts.orig: Article 94
texts.text: Article 94

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Repeal of Directive 95/46/EC
texts.text: Repeal of Directive 95/46/EC

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Directive 95/46/EC is repealed with effect from 25 May 2018.
texts.text: Directive 95/46/EC is repealed with effect from 25 May 2018.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: References to the repealed Directive shall be construed as references to this Regulation. References to the Working Party on the Protection of Individuals with regard to the Processing of Personal Data established by Article 29 of Directive 95/46/EC shall be construed as references to the European Data Protection Board established by this Regulation.
texts.text: References to the repealed Directive shall be construed as references to this Regulation. References to the Working Party on the Protection of Individuals with regard to the Processing of Personal Data established by Article 29 of Directive 95/46/EC shall be construed as references to the European Data Protection Board established by this Regulation.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 95
texts.text: Article 95

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Relationship with Directive 2002/58/EC
texts.text: Relationship with Directive 2002/58/EC

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This  Regulation  shall  not  impose  additional  obligations  on  natural  or  legal  persons  in  relation  to  processing  in connection  with  the  provision  of  publicly  available  electronic  communications  services  in  public  communication networks  in  the  Union  in  relation  to  matters  for  which  they  are  subject  to  specific  obligations  with  the  same  objective set out in Directive 2002/58/EC.
texts.text: This  Regulation  shall  not  impose  additional  obligations  on  natural  or  legal  persons  in  relation  to  processing  in connection  with  the  provision  of  publicly  available  electronic  communications  services  in  public  communication networks  in  the  Union  in  relation  to  matters  for  which  they  are  subject  to  specific  obligations  with  the  same  objective set out in Directive 2002/58/EC.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 96
texts.text: Article 96

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Relationship with previously concluded Agreements
texts.text: Relationship with previously concluded Agreements

texts.label: text
texts.orig: International  agreements  involving  the  transfer  of  personal  data  to  third  countries  or  international  organisations  which were concluded by Member States prior  to 24 May 2016, and which comply with Union law as applicable prior  to that date, shall remain in force until amended, replaced or revoked.
texts.text: International  agreements  involving  the  transfer  of  personal  data  to  third  countries  or  international  organisations  which were concluded by Member States prior  to 24 May 2016, and which comply with Union law as applicable prior  to that date, shall remain in force until amended, replaced or revoked.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 97
texts.text: Article 97

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Commission reports
texts.text: Commission reports

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: By 25 May 2020 and every four years thereafter, the Commission shall submit a report on the evaluation and review of this Regulation to the European Parliament and to the Council. The reports shall be made public.
texts.text: By 25 May 2020 and every four years thereafter, the Commission shall submit a report on the evaluation and review of this Regulation to the European Parliament and to the Council. The reports shall be made public.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In the context of the evaluations and reviews referred to in paragraph 1, the Commission shall examine, in particular, the application and functioning of:
texts.text: In the context of the evaluations and reviews referred to in paragraph 1, the Commission shall examine, in particular, the application and functioning of:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) Chapter V on the transfer of personal data to third countries or international organisations with particular regard to decisions adopted pursuant to Article 45(3) of this Regulation and decisions adopted on the basis of Article 25(6) of Directive 95/46/EC;
texts.text: (a) Chapter V on the transfer of personal data to third countries or international organisations with particular regard to decisions adopted pursuant to Article 45(3) of this Regulation and decisions adopted on the basis of Article 25(6) of Directive 95/46/EC;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) Chapter VII on cooperation and consistency.
texts.text: (b) Chapter VII on cooperation and consistency.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: For the purpose of paragraph 1, the Commission may request information from Member States and supervisory authorities.
texts.text: For the purpose of paragraph 1, the Commission may request information from Member States and supervisory authorities.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In carrying out the evaluations and reviews referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2, the Commission shall take into account the positions and findings of the European Parliament, of the Council, and of other relevant bodies or sources.
texts.text: In carrying out the evaluations and reviews referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2, the Commission shall take into account the positions and findings of the European Parliament, of the Council, and of other relevant bodies or sources.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Commission shall, if necessary, submit appropriate proposals to amend this Regulation, in particular taking into account of developments in information technology and in the light of the state of progress in the information society.
texts.text: The Commission shall, if necessary, submit appropriate proposals to amend this Regulation, in particular taking into account of developments in information technology and in the light of the state of progress in the information society.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 98
texts.text: Article 98

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Review of other Union legal acts on data protection
texts.text: Review of other Union legal acts on data protection

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  Commission  shall,  if  appropriate,  submit  legislative  proposals  with  a  view  to  amending  other  Union  legal  acts  on the  protection  of  personal  data,  in  order  to  ensure  uniform  and  consistent  protection  of  natural  persons  with  regard  to processing.  This  shall  in  particular  concern  the  rules  relating  to  the  protection  of  natural  persons  with  regard  to processing by Union institutions, bodies, offices and agencies and on the free movement of such data.
texts.text: The  Commission  shall,  if  appropriate,  submit  legislative  proposals  with  a  view  to  amending  other  Union  legal  acts  on the  protection  of  personal  data,  in  order  to  ensure  uniform  and  consistent  protection  of  natural  persons  with  regard  to processing.  This  shall  in  particular  concern  the  rules  relating  to  the  protection  of  natural  persons  with  regard  to processing by Union institutions, bodies, offices and agencies and on the free movement of such data.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 99
texts.text: Article 99

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Entry into force and application
texts.text: Entry into force and application

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: This Regulation shall enter into force on the twentieth day following that of its publication in the Official Journal of the European Union .
texts.text: This Regulation shall enter into force on the twentieth day following that of its publication in the Official Journal of the European Union .

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: It shall apply from 25 May 2018.
texts.text: It shall apply from 25 May 2018.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This Regulation shall be binding in its entirety and directly applicable in all Member States.
texts.text: This Regulation shall be binding in its entirety and directly applicable in all Member States.
